      <title>Lightyear: Senior Product Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Remote
</p>

<div class="col col-xs-7 description"><br><p><span><span><span><span><span><span>The Company:</span></span></span></span></span></span><span><span><span><span><span><span> </span></span></span></span></span></span><a href="https://lightyear.ai"><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span>Lightyear</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></a><span><span><span><span><span><span> builds software that is revolutionizing the telecom management experience for hundreds of enterprises. Lightyear’s platform helps enterprise IT teams automate telecom procurement, network inventory management, telecom bill payment, and much more, and is utilized by companies including Honeywell, Alo Yoga, Palo Alto Networks, and Louis Vuitton. Lightyear has raised nearly $50M from the first investors in Roblox, Discord, Coupang, Robinhood, and Flexport.</span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p><span><span><span><span><span><span>The Position: </span></span></span></span></span></span><span><span><span><span><span><span>As a Senior PM at Lightyear, you will be responsible for researching, scoping, designing, and implementing several of our key software features and tools that are disrupting enterprise telecom. A Lightyear PM wears many hats: feature scoping, design, sprint lead, customer growth / conversion (and potentially more based on how you set your role). You will sit at the intersection of customers, GTM, and engineering - coordinating the conception and delivery of our most important product ideas. We’re an early stage company, so your position will play a heavy role in defining a product that customers both need and love.</span></span></span></span></span></span></p><p><span><span><span><span><span><span>This position will be full-time and salaried with competitive cash and equity compensation.&nbsp; The total cash compensation range is estimated to be within $150-180k. This position is fully remote.</span></span></span></span></span></span><br>&nbsp;</p><p><span><span><span><span><span><span>Responsibilities for this role include:</span></span></span></span></span></span></p><ul><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Own the product vision for several key areas of Lightyear’s platform</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Engage with the customer and sales to define and optimize requirements for features</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Coordinate with engineering on feasibility, sprint planning, and shipping product</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Create a first-class UI / UX experience (with help from internal design resources)</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Help define the short, medium, and long term roadmap</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Help implement a data-driven feature discovery and prioritization process</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Coordinate with GTM and Operations to concept and drive success of pilots with prospective customers</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Be a product evangelist - develop product collateral to market product progress</span></span></span></span></span></span><br>&nbsp;</li></ul><p><span><span><span><span><span><span>Our ideal candidate:</span></span></span></span></span></span></p><ul><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>4+ years experience working with enterprise or consumer software technologies as a product manager or software engineer</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Has intense curiosity and passion to deeply understand the customer and problem space, with willingness to dive in the weeds throughout the process</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Experience and enthusiasm taking product or processes from 0 to 1</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Familiarity with software dev practices used to deploy applications and data systems</span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Some design skill (bonus: familiarity with design tools such as Figma, Sketch, etc.)</span></span></span></span></span></span></li></ul><ul><li><span><span><span><span><span><span>Bonus: Familiarity with the telecom industry</span></span></span></span></span></span></li></ul> </div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lightyear-senior-product-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/lightyear-senior-product-manager</a></p>
</description>

    <item>
      <title>Updater: Lead DevOps Engineer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Remote, US
</p>

<div class="job__description body"><div><p></p><br><p>Join Updater’s Platform Engineering team as a Lead DevOps Engineer who combines technical excellence and exceptional collaboration skills to deliver impact. You will drive vital initiatives including deployment velocity, observability, system reliability, and developer experience. You'll build the infrastructure and tooling that lets engineers ship faster and sleep better—think GitOps workflows, Kubernetes configuration and optimization, comprehensive Datadog observability coverage, and the kind of automation that makes deployments uneventful. This is platform engineering done right: pragmatic, impactful, and centered on enabling the broader engineering organization.</p><br><h2>Key Responsibilities</h2><br><ul><br><li>Embody a culture of continuous improvement and shared ownership backed by clear documentation, runbooks, and other shift left contributions</li><br><li>Build strong, collaborative relationships evidenced by proactive engagement from product teams</li><br><li>Continually improve system reliability and observability coverage</li><br><li>Reduce tooling and config complexity so that engineers spend less time fighting tools and more time building features</li><br><li>Optimize Infrastructure costs without compromising reliability</li><br><li>Reduce time-to-production for new features and services</li><br><li>Ensure reliable, repeatable, and better standardized deployments with minimal manual intervention</li><br></ul><br><h2>Must-Have Skills and Experience</h2><br><h4>Soft Skills</h4><br><ul><br><li>Excellent cross-functional collaboration abilities—your work spans the entire engineering organization, requiring you to build and utilize relationships and champion DevEx improvements across teams</li><br><li>Ability to communicate effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences alike, and leverages visuals, data, and concise documentation to build shared understanding</li><br><li>Deep empathy for DevEx—you genuinely understand engineer pain points, involve users in design decisions, and measure success by how much you improve their daily work</li><br><li>Positive, team-oriented mindset that treats every day as an opportunity to improve and inspire others through your can-do attitude</li><br></ul><br><h4>Technical</h4><br><ul><br><li>Previous experience on a platform engineering team where you led complex infrastructure or tooling migration efforts and built useful dev-focused tools</li><br><li>Deep practical experience architecting and implementing solutions in AWS, backed by strong command of cloud design patterns and technologies</li><br><li>Deep expertise in Kubernetes containerized workloads and orchestration</li><br><li>Strong command of observability tools like DataDog or Prometheus</li><br><li>Proficiency in Terraform IaC&nbsp;</li><br><li>Experience in building and maintaining CI/CD pipelines and associated best practices</li><br><li>Solid experience with Git via Gitlab, Github, or Bitbucket.</li><br><li>Familiarity with GitOps tooling like Spacelift or Flux</li><br><li>Strong project management skills that balance interrupt-driven and roadmap work</li><br><li>Eagerness to learn new and evolving technologies in the Platform Engineering ecosystem</li><br></ul><br><h4>Bonus Points For</h4><br><ul><br><li>Frontend or backend development skills</li><br><li>Previous experience as project lead or team lead</li><br><li>Use of AI tooling to help improve efficiency in your daily work</li><br><li>Previous experience tuning SQL and caching technologies</li><br></ul><br><p><br>This posting is anticipated to remain open until 1/31/2026. The new hire salary range for this position is $180,000 - $215,000 annually. Factors which may affect the starting pay within this range include skills, experience, and other qualifications aligned with Updater's internal leveling guidelines.</p></div><div><h2><strong>About Updater</strong></h2><br><p>Updater is a leading technology platform helping major brands acquire and retain customers at scale. We are on a mission to power millions of complex transactions through intelligent, autonomous systems. By combining deep integrations, real-world data, and purpose-built AI agents, we’re transforming fragmented purchasing workflows into seamless, automated experiences – freeing businesses and consumers from operational friction and enabling a new standard of speed, accuracy, and scale.</p><br><p>Updater has raised over $450 million from leading investors, including SoftBank Capital, Vista Credit Partners, IA Ventures, Commerce Ventures, Second Century Ventures (the strategic investment arm of the National Association of Realtors®), and more. For more information, please visit www.updater.com.</p><br><p>Updater is proud to be an equal opportunity employer and will consider all qualified applicants regardless of color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital or family status, disability, gender identity or expression, veteran status, actual or presumed belonging to an ethnic group, or any other legally protected status. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know.</p><br><p><em>Benefits: </em>The Company offers the following benefits for this full-time position, subject to applicable eligibility requirements:</p><br><ul><br><li>Medical, Dental, and Vision Insurance&nbsp;</li><br><li>Flexible PTO</li><br><li>13 paid company holidays annually&nbsp;</li><br><li>Updater Stock Options&nbsp;</li><br><li>401(k) with employer match</li><br><li>Wellbeing Subsidy</li><br><li>One Medical membership</li><br><li>Virtual on-demand healthcare through Teladoc and Talkspace</li><br><li>Flexible spending account (FSA)</li><br><li>Health savings account (HSA)</li><br><li>Supplemental Short &amp; Long Term Disability Insurance&nbsp;</li><br><li>Supplemental Life Insurance</li><br><li>Paid Parental Leave</li><br></ul></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/updater-lead-devops-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/updater-lead-devops-engineer</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Screenpal: Director of Product Marketing</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Seattle, Washington, United States
</p>

<p><strong>Join us!</strong></p><p>ScreenPal’s AI-enhanced video tools make it simple to create compelling videos and amplify your message. Our mission is to help people share their expertise—never replace it. With millions of users worldwide, ScreenPal is a leading SaaS platform empowering educators, trainers, and businesses to learn, teach, and communicate more effectively.</p><p>We’re a profitable, rapidly growing, privately held company with a virtual team and headquarters in Seattle, WA.</p><p>We take pride in delivering exceptional experiences and engaging customers around the globe. At ScreenPal, we build tools that are easy, intuitive, and fun—and our marketing team plays a vital role in bringing that mission to life.</p><p><strong>About the role</strong></p><p>We are seeking an outstanding <strong>Product Marketing Director</strong> to define and drive the go-to-market strategy for our product suite. In this role, you will craft compelling product messaging, champion the voice of our customers, and build programs that fuel adoption, engagement, and loyalty.</p><p>You excel at understanding customer needs, developing high-impact positioning, and leading cross-functional initiatives that accelerate growth. You are a storyteller, a strategic thinker, and a hands-on marketer who thrives in a fast-paced, collaborative environment.</p><p><strong>What you will do:</strong></p><p><strong>Go-to-Market Leadership</strong></p><ul><li>Develop GTM strategy, messaging, and positioning for new products and features across education, training, video marketing, and more. </li><li>Lead cross-functional planning and execution for product launches and major marketing initiatives. </li><li>Drive global product adoption for both B2C self-serve users and B2B Team Plans; recommend new growth opportunities and expansion strategies. </li></ul><p><strong>Demand Generation &amp; Digital Marketing</strong></p><ul><li>Utilize digital channels, virtual events, and webinars to generate demand and accelerate pipeline. </li><li>Build and optimize self-serve acquisition funnels to increase signups, trials, and paid conversions. </li><li>Lead integrated campaigns that generate high-quality leads with measurable ROI. </li><li>Manage global digital programs including social media, email, and content marketing. </li></ul><p><strong>Customer Insights &amp; Storytelling</strong></p><ul><li>Continuously analyze customer needs, market trends, and product performance through both qualitative and quantitative methods. </li><li>Develop story-driven content, customer narratives, and case studies that highlight the impact of our video, image, and collaboration solutions. </li><li>Use video as a core communication medium—internally and externally. </li></ul><p><strong>Cross-Functional Alignment</strong></p><ul><li>Partner closely with engineering, marketing, product, design, and customer support to ensure seamless GTM execution. </li><li>Refine the customer journey and implement lifecycle marketing strategies across onboarding, activation, usage, and renewal. </li><li>Build and manage a strong customer advocacy engine including testimonials, reviews, and evangelist programs. </li></ul><p><strong>Sales Enablement &amp; Market Intelligence</strong></p><ul><li>Equip B2B sales with GTM assets such as pitch decks, demo scripts, ROI models, and competitive battle cards. </li><li>Maintain a clear understanding of the competitive landscape, market trends, and evolving customer pain points to inform product and packaging decisions.</li></ul> <p><strong>Must Have:</strong></p><ul><li>Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business, or related field. </li><li>7+ years of product marketing experience in SaaS (B2B and B2B2C strongly preferred). </li><li>Exceptional storytelling skills; strong writer and editor capable of producing high-quality content. </li><li>Highly data-driven with experience optimizing programs to achieve company-level targets. </li><li>Demonstrated success scaling product-led growth and demand generation programs. </li><li>Strong data visualization skills and the ability to clearly communicate insights and business impact. </li><li>Expertise with CRM systems for lead generation, nurturing, tracking, and reporting. </li><li>Experience with web analytics, A/B testing, email marketing, social media, and other marketing tools. </li><li>Hands-on experience with content marketing, and lifecycle marketing. </li><li>Creative thinker capable of developing compelling customer-facing narratives.</li></ul><p></p><p><strong>Nice to Have:</strong></p><ul><li>Experience with inbound B2B and B2C marketing strategies. </li><li>SaaS experience with free-to-paid conversion models. </li><li>Understanding of the education and training markets. </li><li>Passion for video as a communication and learning tool. </li><li>Collaborative, flexible team player with a sense of humor.</li></ul> <ul><li>Health Care Plan (Medical, Dental &amp; Vision)</li><li>Retirement Plan (401k, IRA)</li><li>Paid Time Off (Vacation, Sick &amp; Public Holidays)</li><li>Work From Home</li></ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/screenpal-director-of-product-marketing">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/screenpal-director-of-product-marketing</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Anteriad: Director, Product Management</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>

      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> India
</p>

<h3><strong>About the Role</strong></h3><p>At Writesonic, customer experience is at the heart of everything we do. As a&nbsp;<strong>Product Support Specialist</strong>, you’ll be the go-to expert helping users get the most out of our platform. You’ll resolve issues, answer questions, and ensure every customer feels supported as they explore our AI SEO and Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) tools.</p><p>This isn’t just a support role—it’s about empowering marketers and businesses worldwide to succeed in the new era of AI-driven search.</p><p></p><h3><strong>About Writesonic</strong></h3><p>Every day, millions turn to AI for answers—and if your brand isn’t there, you’re nowhere. While others scramble to adapt to AI-driven search, Writesonic is already leading the way.</p><p>We’re building the world’s most advanced&nbsp;<strong>AI SEO and Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) platform</strong>—designed to help brands monitor and improve visibility across AI search engines like ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, and Google AI Overviews. Writesonic is the only platform that unifies GEO insights with traditional SEO, giving brands the tools to rank across both Google and emerging AI search platforms.</p><p>Trusted by 20,000+ marketers worldwide, we’re redefining what it means to be discoverable—from Google to AI and beyond.</p><p></p><h3><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></h3><ul><li>Deliver timely, empathetic, and effective support to customers via chat, email, and community channels.</li><li>Troubleshoot product and technical issues, collaborating with engineering and product teams to drive quick resolutions.</li><li>Help customers understand Writesonic’s GEO and SEO capabilities—ensuring they can unlock maximum value.</li><li>Create and maintain documentation: FAQs, knowledge base articles, and best practice guides.</li><li>Proactively identify trends in customer feedback and share insights with the product team.</li><li>Act as the customer’s voice internally, helping shape a better product experience.</li><li>Support onboarding and adoption initiatives to make Writesonic a daily habit for customers.</li></ul><p></p><h3><strong>What We’re Looking For</strong></h3><ul><li>1–3 years in SaaS or tech product support, ideally with exposure to SEO, AI, or marketing tools.</li><li>Excellent communication skills (written &amp; verbal) with the ability to simplify complex concepts.</li><li>Experience using support platforms like Intercom, Zendesk, or Freshdesk.</li><li>Strong problem-solving and analytical mindset; comfortable troubleshooting technical issues.</li><li>Passion for customer experience—empathetic, patient, and proactive.</li><li>A fast learner who thrives in a high-growth, remote-first startup environment.</li></ul><p></p><h3><strong>Why Join as a Product Support Specialist at Writesonic?</strong></h3><ul><li>Play a&nbsp;<strong>critical role in shaping the customer journey</strong>&nbsp;in one of the fastest-growing AI startups.</li><li>Competitive compensation and performance-based incentives.</li><li>Remote-first flexibility with global collaboration.</li><li>Opportunities to grow into customer success, product, or operations roles.</li><li>A supportive, diverse team that values learning and celebrates wins together.</li></ul><p></p><p> If you’re passionate about problem-solving, customer experience, and helping brands succeed in the AI search era, we’d love to have you on our support team at</p> 

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/writesonic-product-support-specialist">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/writesonic-product-support-specialist</a></p>
</description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> United States
</p>

<p>Key Responsibilities:</p><p></p><p>Design, implement, and manage CI/CD pipelines for application deployment.</p><p>Develop and maintain cloud infrastructure primarily in AWS, with secondary responsibilities in Azure, using IaC tools like Terraform or CloudFormation.</p><p>Manage containerized applications using Docker and orchestration platforms like Kubernetes (EKS/AKS).</p><p>Implement and manage monitoring, logging, and alerting solutions to ensure platform reliability and performance.</p><p>Collaborate with development teams to optimize application architecture for the cloud.</p><p>Ensure the security and compliance of the cloud infrastructure.</p><p>Core Qualifications:</p><p></p><p></p> <p>Extensive AWS Experience (80%): Proven, hands-on experience with core AWS services such as EC2, S3, VPC, IAM, RDS, and Lambda. Deep knowledge of AWS networking and security.</p><p>Microsoft Azure Experience (20%): Demonstrable experience with foundational Azure services, including Azure VMs, Blob Storage, Azure AD, and VNet.</p><p>Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Proficiency with tools like Terraform (preferred), CloudFormation, or Ansible.</p><p>CI/CD Tools: Strong experience with Jenkins, GitLab CI, Azure DevOps, or similar.</p><p>Containerization: Solid understanding of Docker and Kubernetes (EKS or AKS).</p><p>Scripting: Competency in a scripting language such as Python, Bash, or PowerShell.</p>

      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Sofia, Sofia City Province, Bulgaria
</p>

<p>emerchantpay is a leading global payment service provider and acquirer for online, mobile, in-store and over the phone payments. Our global payments solution is available through a simple integration, offering a diverse range of features, including global acquiring, global and local payment methods, advanced fraud management and performance optimization. We empower businesses to design seamless and engaging payment experiences for their consumers.</p><p>We are looking for a <strong>Senior DevOps Engineer</strong> to join our team!</p><h3>Responsibilities</h3><ul><li>Automating infrastructure with tools such as Terraform, Puppet, Ansible and similar;</li><li>Managing 100+ Linux servers in a PCI-DSS level environment (service provider level 1);</li><li>Overall responsibility for technical operations and monitoring on different layers;</li><li>Analysing, troubleshooting and resolving problems.</li></ul><h3>Requirements</h3><ul><li>2-3 years of experience building infrastructure with automation tools, like Terraform, Puppet, Ansible;</li><li>Successful experience with major Cloud Provider such as AWS, GCP, Azure;</li><li>5-10 years of experience in Linux administration;</li><li>Experience with Bash, Python or Ruby;</li><li>Automation with Jenkins, GIT knowledge;</li><li>Experience with databases like MySQL, MariaDB, Galera, AWS RDS, AWS Aurora, Cloud Spanner;</li><li>Experienced user of virtualisation, and VMWare environments in particular;</li><li>IT-related degree or similar;</li><li>Highly proficient in spoken and written English;</li><li>An enthusiastic, hard-working, motivated person with excellent communication skills;</li><li>Considered as an advantage: Development experience, strong networking background - proven experience in a HA, PCI-DSS environment and experience in payments and payment-based infrastructures.</li></ul><h3>Benefits</h3><ul><li>Fast-growing payment company;</li><li>Excellent working conditions, casual atmosphere, and state-of-the-art hardware;</li><li>Modern, challenging, constantly growing business;</li><li>Professional development - books, trainings, certifications, etc.;</li><li>Team buildings and fun activities;</li><li>25 days paid holiday, 1 day for every 2 years with us;</li><li>Fully distributed and remote.</li></ul><p><strong>If you are interested, please apply with your CV in English. Only short-listed candidates will be contacted.</strong></p><p>Personal data of the applicants will be processed in strict confidentiality by emerchantpay Ltd., UIC 175117520 solely for the purposes of selection and recruitment and will not be transferred to other data controllers unless required by law. Applicants provide their personal data on a voluntary basis and will have the right to access and correct their personal data within a reasonable time upon filing a written request.</p><p>emerchantpay is an equal opportunity employer. We appreciate people with different backgrounds and mindsets, and we honor diversity and inclusion.<br></p> 

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/emerchantpay-senior-devops-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/emerchantpay-senior-devops-engineer</a></p>
</description>

    <item>
      <title>Tabnine: Senior Product Marketer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> US (Remote), Israel
</p>

<p><strong>A bit about us:</strong></p><p>Since launching the industry’s first AI coding assistant in 2018, Tabnine has pioneered generative AI to elevate the coding experience for over a million developers worldwide across thousands of companies. Our mission is to help development teams of every size speed up and simplify the software development process with AI, while maintaining standards of privacy and security. Tabnine boosts engineering velocity, code quality, and developer happiness by automating the coding workflow through AI tools customized to their teams. Unlike generic coding assistants, Tabnine is the AI that you control. We’re looking for top-tier talent to join the Tabnine team and we’d love to hear from you!</p><p><strong>About the Role</strong></p><p>This role is for a product marketer who thrives at the intersection of technology and storytelling — someone who can turn complex AI and developer tools into clear, compelling narratives that move markets. You’ll own how our products and category are positioned, crafting bold messaging that resonates with both technical and executive audiences. One day you’ll be writing a whitepaper or launching a campaign, the next you’ll be demoing live to developers or presenting strategy to leadership. It’s a fast-paced, high-impact role for someone with startup energy, creative drive, and the technical fluency to shape the future of AI-powered software development.</p><p>You’re not the “wait for a brief” type. You see a launch opportunity, a trend, or a gap — and you’re already halfway through the Google Doc. You live for that sweet spot where <strong>technology, storytelling, and impact collide</strong>.</p><p>You can talk LLM token usage one minute, then turn around and craft a killer headline or whitepaper the next. You’re just as comfortable demoing to a room of developers as you are presenting a GTM plan to leadership. You get that great marketing isn’t about buzzwords — it’s about <strong>clarity, proof, and momentum</strong>.</p><p>You’ve got a bias for action, a hunger for AI, and the creative nerve to try things others overthink.</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p><ul><li><strong>Own positioning &amp; messaging</strong> - shape how the world understands our products and the category we’re creating.</li><li><strong>Tell the story</strong> - translate complex tech into simple, bold narratives that make people feel something.</li><li><strong>Launch with impact</strong> - plan and execute GTM campaigns, product launches, and partner integrations that move the needle.</li><li><strong>Be the voice</strong> - host webinars, join podcasts, lead product demos, and represent us at events.</li><li><strong>Create fast, think faster</strong> - whitepapers, videos, landing pages, blog posts — you can spin up assets in hours, not weeks.</li><li><strong>Connect with developers</strong> - get hands-on with our product, build quick demos, and show devs what’s possible.</li><li><strong>Collaborate cross-functionally</strong> - work closely with Product, Demand Gen, and Sales to bring ideas to life and drive adoption.</li></ul> <p><strong>You’ll Thrive Here If You:</strong></p><ul><li>Love <strong>talking tech</strong> - APIs, agents, AI, IDEs, LLMs… it’s your playground.</li><li>Have <strong>startup energy</strong> - you build, ship, learn, and iterate fast.</li><li>Believe in <strong>clarity over corporate</strong> - you can explain a complex feature in 10 words or less.</li><li>Are <strong>comfortable on camera and stage</strong> - you enjoy telling the story live.</li><li>Want to <strong>shape a category</strong>, not just sell a product.</li><li>Can move between <strong>strategic and scrappy</strong> without missing a beat.</li></ul><p><strong>Your Background Might Include:</strong></p><ul><li>5–8 years in Product Marketing or Dev Marketing at a tech or developer-focused company. Bonus points if you have a technical background or education.</li><li>Proven experience launching products, running GTMs, and working directly with technical audiences.</li><li>Strong writing and storytelling skills — you can crank out crisp content fast.</li><li>Confidence with webinars, demos, and speaking engagements.</li><li>Curiosity about (or hands-on with) AI, coding tools, or modern dev workflows.</li></ul><p><strong>Why You’ll Love It Here</strong></p><ul><li>You’ll market <strong>cutting-edge AI</strong> that actually changes how developers build.</li><li>You’ll work with a passionate team that values <strong>speed, creativity, and ownership.</strong></li><li>You’ll get to experiment - and you’ll have the runway to make bold moves.</li><li>You’ll help define the next big category in AI-powered software development.</li></ul><p><strong>Ready to build the story behind the future of AI coding?</strong></p><p>Let’s talk.</p>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/tabnine-senior-product-marketer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/tabnine-senior-product-marketer</a></p>
</description>

    <item>
      <title>Openmined: Senior Technical Product Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> United States
</p>

<p>We’re looking for a Senior Technical Product Manager to join OpenMined, a nonprofit on a mission to connect the world’s non-public, siloed data into a shared network - unlocking 1,000,000× more knowledge across every scientific field and industry, while ensuring it is offered to the world as a part of an open, public information network.</p><p>If the world moves toward a future where private data can be shared responsibly instead of locked inside corporate silos, it will be partly because of the strategy you helped shape - one that empowers engineers, researchers, and institutions to collaborate without giving up control. The products and systems you help build will play a defining role in enabling this once-in-a-generation technological shift and fueling a new wave of innovation.</p><p>You’ll be joining a small, highly technical, early-stage team working before product-market fit - where every decision matters and nothing is fully defined. You’ll lead the exhilarating process of discovery and iteration that turns cutting-edge privacy and AI research into products that data owners and data scientists actually love to use. You’ll work side by side with a deeply skilled, mission-driven group of engineers and researchers, diving deep into code when needed, defining what success looks like, and driving momentum through uncertainty.</p><p>If you’re the kind of person who gets energized by impossible-sounding problems, thrives when the path isn’t clear, and wants to leave a mark on how the world shares knowledge - this is the place to do it. Bring your grit, your curiosity, and your taste for impact. Let’s build the future of data together.</p><p></p><h3>Who you are</h3><p>You’re a builder at heart - someone who doesn’t wait for clarity but creates it. You’ve probably worked in messy, early-stage environments where direction was fuzzy, timelines were tight, and outcomes depended on your ability to turn chaos into momentum. You love digging into complex technical problems, understanding them deeply, and translating them into products that actually work for real users.</p><p>You don’t need a big team or a perfect roadmap to make progress - just a mission that matters and smart people to build with. You’re equally comfortable brainstorming a product vision, debugging a workflow, or jumping into a user call to understand what’s not clicking.&nbsp;</p><p>Most of all, you’re motivated by purpose. You want to use your skills to build something that makes the world more open, fair, and capable of solving its hardest problems.</p><p></p><h3>Responsibilities</h3><ul><li>Drive discovery, experimentation, and delivery to take Syft from concept to product-market fit serving passionate users.</li><li>Conduct user research and go-to-market experiments to uncover workflows, pain points, and incentives that guide product direction.</li><li>Collaborate closely with engineers and researchers to define, build, and iterate on deeply technical products that unlock new ways to collaborate on private data.</li><li>Translate complex systems - privacy-preserving computations, federated architectures, LLM connectors, and more - into intuitive user experiences.</li><li>Immerse yourself in the evolving landscape of privacy, AI, and data collaboration to anticipate shifts in technology, incentives, and regulation.</li><li>Roll up your sleeves when speed matters - prototype flows, fix bugs or even code alongside the team to accelerate learning.</li><li>Identify the signals that matter - from activation to retention to mission alignment - and use them to guide iteration.</li><li>Communicate priorities with clarity, aligning a distributed team on near-term execution and long-term mission.</li><li>Champion the voice of our users so our most technical work stays grounded in real human problems.</li><li>Engage with policy and standards communities to ensure privacy-preserving technologies are adopted responsibly.</li><li>Cultivate strategic partnerships with organizations whose data, expertise, or reach can accelerate our mission.</li></ul> <ul><li>3+ years of product management experience - building or scaling technical products - ideally in environments without a clear playbook.</li><li>Strong technical foundation with proven experience coding and collaborating closely with engineers to ship complex software. Able to reason about distributed systems, privacy technologies, and AI.</li><li>Ability to bridge research and implementation - turning technical possibilities into practical product directions, with a deep curiosity about emerging technologies (i.e. secure computation, RAG, mixture of experts).</li><li>Comfort in the unknown - thrive in ambiguity, create structure, and bring clarity where others see complexity.</li><li>Hands-on with code and tooling - able to read PRs, debug, or prototype ideas to help the team move faster.</li><li>Exceptional communicator - clear in writing and speech, able to align distributed teams and build trust across engineers, researchers, policymakers, and partners.</li><li>Ability to build trusted relationships across diverse stakeholder groups, i.e. researchers, engineers, policymakers, and partners.</li><li>Mission-driven mindset - you care deeply about how technology shapes society, and you’re excited by OpenMined’s goal of unlocking the world’s non-public information.</li></ul><p></p><h3>Nice-to-Haves</h3><ul><li>Prior experience in early-stage or research-to-product environments.</li><li>Experience as a data scientist or building for data scientists (including experience using Colab and Jupyter)</li><li>Contributions to open-source projects or standards efforts.</li><li>Exposure/curiosity about public policy, privacy, or technology governance.</li><li>Entrepreneurial or founder-like experience - especially where you had to build both product and ecosystem from scratch.</li></ul> <ul><li>Mission-Driven Work: Be part of a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization focused on social impact rather than profit, working toward a safer, more privacy-preserving AI ecosystem.</li><li>Open Source Contribution: Make a real impact by contributing to tools that empower global collaboration.</li><li>Collaborative Global Team: Join a small, ambitious, and international team that values clarity, candor, and creativity.</li><li>Flexible Work Hours: Structure your work around what best fits your life and productivity.</li><li>Fully Remote: Work from anywhere - no commute, no office politics.</li><li>Results-Driven Culture: We value autonomy, accountability, and results over bureaucracy.</li><li>Flexible Paid Time Off: Take time as you need it.</li><li>Competitive Compensation: Receive fair and transparent pay, equitable across regions.</li></ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/openmined-senior-technical-product-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/openmined-senior-technical-product-manager</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Privy: Head of Product Marketing</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Austin, Texas, United States
</p>

<p>We’re seeking a Head of Product Marketing who will own product positioning, messaging, and go-to-market strategy across our two platforms: Privy and Emotive. You’ll work closely with Product, Sales, and Customer Success to deeply understand our customers, drive adoption, and tell compelling product stories that convert.</p><h3>What You'll Do</h3><ul><li>Own positioning and messaging for new and existing product features across email, SMS, and on-site conversion tools.</li><li>Partner with Product and Engineering to launch new features, own the GTM process, and support adoption.</li><li>Develop competitive insights and articulate differentiated value across customer segments.</li><li>Create and maintain product collateral, including landing pages, sales decks, one-pagers, and in-app messaging.</li><li>Conduct and synthesize customer and market research to inform positioning and roadmap.</li><li>Support the Sales and Success teams with enablement tools and product training.</li><li>Collaborate with the Growth and Lifecycle teams to fuel campaigns and product education.</li><li>Work cross-functionally to ensure our brand voice and positioning are consistent across all channels.</li></ul> <ul><li>5-7 years of product marketing experience in SaaS, ideally in e-commerce or martech.</li><li>You’re a strong writer who can translate technical capabilities into clear customer value.</li><li>You thrive in scrappy, fast-paced startup environments and enjoy building from zero to one.</li><li>You’re analytical, customer-obsessed, and skilled at turning insights into action.</li><li>You’re comfortable leading GTM efforts and working cross-functionally across teams.</li><li>Bonus if you’ve worked with Shopify brands, DTC founders, or e-commerce platforms.</li></ul> <ul> <li>High autonomy with no career ceilings.</li> <li>Challenging and interesting work.</li> <li>Amazing colleagues.</li> <li>Competitive salary and equity.</li> <li>Work remotely.</li> <li>Covered health, dental, and vision insurance.</li> <li>Regular team events and off-sites.</li> <li>Unlimited PTO and generous paid parental leave.</li> </ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/privy-head-of-product-marketing">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/privy-head-of-product-marketing</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Crewbloom: Sales and Marketing Specialist</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> India
</p>

<p>We seek an experienced and dynamic Sales and Marketing Specialist to join our client’s team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in e-commerce marketing, specifically within the print-on-demand industry, and a proven track record of driving sales and increasing brand visibility. This role requires expertise in digital marketing strategies, social media management, email marketing, SEO, customer relationship management, and influencer marketing, including liaising with agencies representing influencers.</p><p></p><p><strong>Job Responsibilities</strong></p><ul> <li>Develop and implement comprehensive sales and marketing strategies to drive traffic, increase conversions, and maximize revenue for the print-on-demand store.</li> <li>Manage and optimize online advertising campaigns (Google Ads, Facebook Ads, Instagram Ads) to ensure a high return on investment.</li> <li>Create, execute, and monitor email marketing campaigns, including newsletters, promotional offers, and product announcements.</li> <li>Oversee social media accounts, developing content strategies that engage followers, build brand awareness, and drive sales.</li> <li>Conduct market research to identify trends, customer preferences, and competitive landscape to inform marketing strategies.</li> <li>Optimize the Shopify store for SEO, ensuring all product listings, blog posts, and web pages rank highly on search engines.</li> <li>Develop and manage in-store promotions, including discounts, referral programs, and affiliate programs to attract and retain customers.</li> <li>Collaborate with the design team to create visually appealing marketing materials, including banners, ads, and social media posts.</li> <li>Develop and manage influencer marketing campaigns, including liaising with influencer agencies, identifying key influencers, and coordinating product promotions.</li> <li>Analyze sales data and marketing metrics to measure the effectiveness of campaigns and adjust strategies as needed.</li> <li>Develop partnerships and collaborations with influencers, bloggers, and other relevant entities to expand brand reach.</li> <li>Handle customer relationship management, ensuring excellent customer service and promptly addressing any issues or inquiries.</li> <li>Monitor and report on industry trends and advancements, recommending new tools and strategies to stay ahead of the competition.</li> </ul> <ul> <li>Proven experience as a Sales and Marketing Specialist, preferably in the e-commerce or print-on-demand industry.</li> <li>Strong understanding of digital marketing principles, including SEO, PPC, social media marketing, email marketing, and content marketing.</li> <li>Proficiency with marketing tools and platforms such as Google Analytics, Google Ads,</li> <li>Facebook Ads Manager, Mailchimp, Hootsuite, and similar.</li> <li>Experience with Shopify and its marketing integrations, apps, and tools.</li> <li>Experience in developing and managing in-store promotions, such as discounts, referral programs, and affiliate programs.</li> <li>Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively with team members and external partners.</li> <li>Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret data and make data-driven decisions.</li> <li>Experience in influencer marketing, including liaising with influencer agencies and coordinating product promotions.</li> <li>Creative thinking and problem-solving abilities to develop innovative marketing campaigns and solutions.</li> <li>Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.</li> <li>Knowledge of design principles and experience working with graphic designers to create marketing materials.</li> <li>Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.</li> </ul><p></p><p><em><strong>Preferred Qualifications:</strong></em></p><ul> <li>Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Business, Communications, or a related field.</li> <li>Experience in influencer marketing and managing affiliate programs.</li> <li>Familiarity with additional e-commerce platforms and tools.</li> <li>Knowledge of HTML/CSS for minor website updates and optimizations.</li> <li>Certification in Google Analytics, Google Ads, or other relevant marketing certifications.</li> <li>Experience with software and tools specific to sales and marketing, such as HubSpot, Klaviyo, SEMrush, Ahrefs, and Canva.</li> </ul><p></p><p><strong>Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:</strong></p><ul> <li> <strong>Internet Connection:</strong> </li> <ul> <li> Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of <strong>15 Mbps</strong>. </li> <li> Backup internet connection with at least <strong>10 Mbps</strong>. </li> <li> Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage. </li> </ul> </ul><p></p><ul> <li> <strong>Primary Device:</strong> </li> <ul> <li> Desktop or laptop equipped with at least: </li> <ul> <li> <strong>Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer)</strong>, <strong>Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer)</strong>, <strong>AMD Ryzen 5</strong>, or an equivalent processor. </li> <li> A minimum of <strong>8 GB RAM</strong>. </li> </ul> </ul> </ul><p></p><ul> <li> <strong>Backup Device:</strong> </li> <ul> <li> Must meet or exceed the performance of an <strong>Intel Core i3</strong> processor. </li> <li> Must be functional during power interruptions. </li> </ul> </ul><p></p><ul> <li> <strong>Peripherals and Workspace:</strong> </li> <ul> <li> A functioning <strong>webcam</strong>. </li> <li> A <strong>noise-canceling USB headset</strong>. </li> <li> A <strong>quiet, dedicated home office space</strong>. </li> <li> A <strong>smartphone</strong> for communication and verification purposes.</li> </ul> </ul> <ul> <li> <strong>Join Our Dynamic Team:</strong> Experience our fun, inclusive, innovative culture that values your unique contributions and supports your professional growth.</li> <li> <strong>Embrace the Opportunities: </strong>Seize daily chances to learn, innovate, and excel. Make a real impact in your field.</li> <li> <strong>Limitless Career Growth: </strong>Unlock a world of possibilities and resources to propel your career forward.</li> <li> <strong>Fast-Paced Thrills:</strong> Thrive in a high-energy, engaging atmosphere. Embrace challenges and reap stimulating rewards.</li> <li> <strong>Flexibility, Your Way:</strong> Embrace the freedom to work from home or any location of your choice. Create your ideal work environment.</li> <li> <strong>Work-Life Balance at Its Best:</strong> Say goodbye to stressful commutes and hello to quality time with loved ones. Achieve a healthy work-life integration to perform at your best.</li> </ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/crewbloom-sales-and-marketing-specialist">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/crewbloom-sales-and-marketing-specialist</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Spectrum It Recruitment: Senior DevOps Engineer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Oxfordshire
</p>

<div class="job-details_jobDescription__1dErB job-details_jobSection__1snwj"><div> <p><strong>A Marketplace on a Mission to the Moon…</strong></p> <p>We are working with an exciting business on a mission to put themselves at the forefront of digital marketplace consumer services. To rival the corporate, and to put the little people - the merchant and the consumer - at the forefront of business success.</p> <p>Having recently been named as Europe's fastest growing ecommerce company by the financial times and having accumulated many accolades from the tech and marketplace industries, the company is ready to scale. </p> <p>Come and join as the company take their marketplace to a global market. </p> <p><strong>Senior DevOps Engineer</strong></p> <p>A crucial role in streamlining software delivery pipelines, enhancing reliability, performance and scalability of systems, and driving continuous improvement across the software lifecycle.</p> <p>There are no passengers in this engineering team, everyone contributes and makes an impact. Your contribution will be significant and will help the ship run smoothly and reach further.</p> <p><strong>What will make you a success in this role?</strong></p> <p>You will need to be experienced, or at least comfortable with, working in a start-up style environment; fast moving, fast thinking, and willing to roll up your sleeves and get stuck in!</p> <ul> <li>Senior level technical capability with strong problem solving skills.</li><li>Proven experience with GCP as either a DevOps Engineer or SRE.</li><li>A good depth of experience using Terraform and Ansible.</li><li>A strong knowledge of Kubernetes cluster, building containers, deploying via CI/CD pipelines.</li><li>Able to think commercially i.e. cost of scaling vs utilisation of existing infrastructure. </li></ul> <p><strong>Beneficial Experience</strong></p> <ul> <li>Knowledge of Nginx, PHP, MySQL.</li><li>Knowledge of NoSQL solutions, RabbitMQ, SOLR, Couchbase.</li><li>Good understanding of Networking concepts and solutions.</li><li>Ability to work with, devise, and implement CI/CD pipelines and practices.</li><li>A solid understanding of networking concepts, technologies, and protocols (TCP/IP, IPSec, HTTPS, DNS).</li></ul> <p>The company have a remote first working practice so this position is available on a remote working basis. If you prefer working in an office with a team of people, they have a fully kitted, modern office based near Bournemouth in Dorset. You're welcome as  often as you like.</p> <p>Salary, approx. £75,000 - £90,000 + benefits, remote working, generous benefits</p> <p>Hit apply to upload your CV or contact Tom Rayner at Spectrum IT Recruitment for more info.</p> <p>Spectrum IT Recruitment (South) Limited is acting as an Employment Agency in relation to this vacancy.</p> </div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/spectrum-it-recruitment-senior-devops-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/spectrum-it-recruitment-senior-devops-engineer</a></p>
</description>

      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Mexico City
</p>

<div class="content"><div class="section-wrapper page-full-width"><div class="section page-centered"><div>Restaurant365 is a SaaS company disrupting the restaurant industry! Our cloud-based platform provides a&nbsp;unique, centralized solution for accounting and back-office operations for restaurants. Restaurant365’s culture is focused on empowering team members to produce top-notch results while elevating their skills. We’re constantly evolving and improving to make sure we are and always will be “Best in Class” ... and we want that for you too!</div><div><br></div><div><span>At Restaurant365, we’re revolutionizing the restaurant industry by creating intuitive, user-centric solutions that make managing and growing restaurants easier. As a Product Designer, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the user experience for restaurant professionals, ensuring that our products not only look great but also solve real-world problems. This role is ideal for designers looking to take their skills to the next level in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. &nbsp;</span></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>How you'll add value: </h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li><b>Design Impactful Experiences</b>: Lead design efforts on small to medium-sized projects, creating thoughtful visual design, interaction design, and high-fidelity prototypes that enhance the user journey. &nbsp;</li><li><b>Collaborate Cross-Functionally:</b> Work closely with product managers, engineers, and other stakeholders to bring your designs to life, ensuring alignment with product goals and business objectives. &nbsp;</li><li><b>Participate in Design Reviews</b>: Present your work to team members and stakeholders, incorporating feedback to iterate and improve designs, always with an eye toward the user’s needs. &nbsp;</li><li><b>Contribute to User-Centered Solutions</b>: Utilize user research, feedback, and data analysis to ensure your designs solve real pain points and elevate the overall product experience. &nbsp;</li><li><b>Enhance Design Consistency</b>: Maintain and contribute to Restaurant365’s design system to ensure cohesive user experiences across all platform components. &nbsp;</li><li>Other duties as assigned.&nbsp;</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>What you'll need to be successful in this role: </h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>3+ years of professional experience in product design, UX/UI, or a related field, with a strong portfolio that demonstrates your problem-solving and user-focused design skills. &nbsp;</li><li>A keen interest in creating intuitive, user-friendly designs that tackle complex problems with creativity and precision. &nbsp;</li><li>Strong expertise in design tools such as Figma, Sketch, or Adobe Creative Suite, with the ability to produce high-quality wireframes, mockups, and interactive prototypes. &nbsp;</li><li>Ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams and value the contributions of product managers, engineers, and fellow designers in the design process. &nbsp;</li><li>A deep understanding of the importance of user research and usability testing, along with the ability to iterate based on feedback and data. &nbsp;</li><li>Comfort working in a fast-paced environment with multiple projects, shifting priorities as necessary to meet business and user needs. &nbsp;</li><div><br></div><div><b>PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS&nbsp;</b></div><li>Prior experience designing for SaaS platforms or B2B products, especially in the restaurant or hospitality industries, is highly desirable. &nbsp;</li><li>In-depth understanding of UX/UI principles and best practices to ensure that designs are functional, elegant, and easy to use. &nbsp;</li><li>Experience contributing to or maintaining design systems to ensure consistency and efficiency across product lines. &nbsp;</li><li>Ability to distill complex user needs and business goals into clean, functional, and aesthetically pleasing design solutions. &nbsp;</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><span>DYN365, Inc d/b/a Restaurant365 is an equal opportunity employer.</span></div></div></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/restaurant365-product-designer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/restaurant365-product-designer</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Method Grid: Product Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Bath, Avon
</p>

<div class="job-details_jobDescription__1dErB job-details_jobSection__1snwj"><div><p><strong>Background to Role</strong></p><p>Method Grid is a scale-up SaaS business with a prestigious client base and serious ambition. </p><p>Our platform helps organisations connect knowledge, process and delivery - turning playbooks into live, auditable workflows that teams actually use. From capital infrastructure projects to renewable energy, you’ll see the impact of your work in places where assurance, compliance and repeatable quality really matter. </p><p>As we continually develop the product (including a raft of new AI-enabled capabilities), we’re creating this role so you can help us lead of our product function and help us deliver more value, more often.</p><p><strong>About You</strong></p><p>You love turning messy, real-world problems into simple, useful outcomes. You get close to customers, listen hard and <em>synthesise</em> what you learn into sharp problem statements and measurable goals. </p><p>You keep a tidy backlog, make trade-offs clear and write crisp stories and acceptance criteria that teams can act on. </p><p>You’re at your best collaborating with Design and Engineering - curious, pragmatic, low-ego - and you use data to guide decisions, whether that’s reading funnels and cohorts or pulling a quick SQL query to answer a question. </p><p>You think about quality early - performance, reliability, security and auditability - and you communicate clearly, keeping people aligned with short, regular updates. Experience in B2B SaaS is important; familiarity with assurance/compliance, governance or reporting is a bonus.</p><p><strong>The Role</strong></p><p>You’ll work closely with our Chief Product and Technology Officer to agree what success looks like, then run lightweight, continuous discovery with customers and colleagues to keep us focused on what matters. </p><p>You’ll define simple scope - PRDs, user stories and acceptance criteria - so delivery stays clear and fast. </p><p>You’ll prioritise the roadmap for impact, effort and risk, keep it honest as things change, and work hand-in-hand with Design and Engineering to ship small, frequent improvements. </p><p>You’ll instrument analytics, learn from experiments and iterate quickly. Along the way, you’ll champion non-functional needs and build in the controls and audit trails our customers expect. </p><p>When it’s time to ship, you’ll co-ordinate releases, enable the team with “what’s changed”, and loop feedback straight back into the next iteration.</p><p><strong>Why Join?</strong></p><p>Method Grid is an early-stage SaaS platform with an ever-growing list of impressive customers. We’ve  secured significant investment both to accelerate the build of our product and to hire a team of passionate experts to deliver an outstanding customer experience.</p><p>It’s going to be an exciting journey.  We believe we have a unique product that will only get better.</p><p>We celebrate diversity and how it can empower a high-performing team. But the one thing that does unify us is our values. As we grow, we intend to keep our values front-&amp;-centre.</p><p>We have a great (and growing) team of co-workers who embody the cultural values of:</p><ul><li><strong>&gt;Team</strong> (highly collaborative)</li><li><strong>Central Rock</strong> (client-centricity)</li><li><strong>Never Settle</strong> (never ending pursuit of excellence coupled with humility to recognise we never get there)</li><li><strong>Egos at the Door</strong> (take our work seriously but not ourselves)</li><li><strong>Fast Craft</strong> (ruthless speed when it comes to product development)</li></ul></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/method-grid-product-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/method-grid-product-manager</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Openprovider: Product Marketing Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> India Remote
</p>

<div class="description-wrapper description-wrapper-shown" lang="en"><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">About the company</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br>                        Openprovider is an ICANN-accredited domain registrar and technology company founded in 2004 in Rotterdam, Netherlands. We are a fully-remote organisation with more than 100 team members spread across 20+ countries. Remote working means no office, no painful commuting, and no stressful traffic - all you need is yourself, a laptop, and a cup of coffee!<br><br>A diverse and inclusive organisation, we support a healthy work/life balance and are constantly looking for new ways to help the well-being of our people. We support a flexible schedule and are 100% performance and result-oriented. At Openprovider, we're not just a company; we're a mission-driven team dedicated to transforming the domain industry. We believe in providing a trusted digital identity to every business.&nbsp;<br><br>Our innovative approach is reshaping the industry from a traditional transactional model to a more customer-centric, subscription-based approach. We pay subscriptions for music, newspapers, and movies - why should domains be any different?<br><br>Come join us on our journey to create a trusted digital world.<br>                    </div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">About the role</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br>                            We are looking for an experienced Product Marketing Manager who will create and deliver compelling narratives that showcase our product's unique value, engaging customers, attracting prospects, and aligning internal teams. You will drive business growth through strategic go-to-market (GTM) plans that capitalise on market opportunities.<br><br>We are full of ideas and energy. We are always on the lookout for new technology and innovative ways to represent Openprovider’s core values and bring our mission to life. We are relentless in our pursuit to deliver increasingly effective and engaging campaigns, articles, and webinars across all our platforms.<br>                        </div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">Key responsibilities</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br><ul><li>Regularly conduct and update market research.</li><li>Analyse customer feedback and product usage data.</li><li>Monitor competitor activities and strategies.</li><li>Create and refine customer segmentation models.</li><li>Develop messaging frameworks for different customer segments.</li><li>Position products to highlight unique value propositions and advantages.</li><li>Produce sales materials, including pitch decks, datasheets, and FAQs.[both internally and externally(for customers)]</li><li>Provide ongoing support and updates to sales and product teams.</li><li>Collaborate with product management to define feature priorities.</li><li>Gather and integrate customer feedback into product development and/or sales enablement.</li><li>Communicate roadmap updates to internal stakeholders.</li><li>Evaluate product performance and market alignment.</li><li>Identify gaps and opportunities for new products or enhancements.</li><li>Align portfolio management with strategic business objectives.</li><li>Collaborate with strategic sales to set and adjust pricing.</li><li>Analyse market pricing trends and customer price sensitivity.</li><li>Use pricing as a strategic tool in product positioning.</li><li>Provide customer insights and feedback to the product team.</li><li>Facilitate customer advisory boards or focus groups.</li><li>Provide strategic insights to guide long-term business decisions.</li><li>Publish regular reports or newsletters on market trends and innovations.</li></ul><br></div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">What we are looking for</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br><ul><li>3+ years of experience as a Product Marketing Manager in Hosting/Domains/SSL or SaaS&nbsp;industry;</li><li>B2B experience;</li><li>Experience of working in international teams;</li><li>Fluent English;</li><li>BSc or MSc in Marketing or a relevant field;</li><li>Proven experience in market research, customer feedback usage, and competitor analysis;</li><li>Affinity in developing customer segmentation models and messaging frameworks;</li><li>Expertise in positioning products strategically in the market;</li><li>Ability to work closely with sales and product management teams;</li><li>Capability to communicate product roadmap updates clearly to stakeholders;</li><li>Experience in facilitating customer advisory boards and focus groups.</li><li>Competence in defining and prioritising product features;</li><li>Expertise in analysing market pricing trends;</li><li>Skill in using pricing as a strategic tool in product positioning;</li><li>Proficiency in presenting strategic insights to guide business decisions.</li></ul><br></div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">What we offer</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br><ul><li>100% remote work (you can work from any location, no need to go to the office);</li><li>Paid time off and sick leave;</li><li>An international team and regular online and offline events to stay connected.</li><li>Internal workshops and knowledge-sharing sessions;</li><li>Quarterly review and annual salary review;</li><li>Flexible working hours (We are a result-oriented company);</li><li>Budget for learning;</li><li>We are an ISO 27001-certified remote workplace.</li></ul><br></div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">About us</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br>                        Openprovider is a wholesaler of domains and internet security products. We provide a platform for domain resellers that allows them to easily manage large numbers of domain names for an affordable price. Our people always come first for us - and that includes both our customers and our colleagues. We believe in the power of collaboration and working together to achieve the best results, and we pride ourselves on our flexible work culture and result-oriented approach.<br><br>We have been fully remote since 2020, and our team has only expanded since then. Our team currently consists of 110+ employees, who are spread across 20+ different countries and 4 different continents. We are proud of our fun and diverse team and regularly connect with each other through online and offline events.<br>                    </div><br><br></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/openprovider-product-marketing-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/openprovider-product-marketing-manager</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Zeta Global: Senior Product Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Remote - United States
</p>

<div class=""><br><p><strong>WHO WE ARE&nbsp;</strong></p><br><p>Zeta Global (NYSE: ZETA) is the AI-Powered Marketing Cloud that leverages advanced artificial intelligence (AI) and trillions of consumer signals to make it easier for marketers to acquire, grow, and retain customers more efficiently. Through the Zeta Marketing Platform (ZMP), our vision is to make sophisticated marketing simple by unifying identity, intelligence, and omnichannel activation into a single platform – powered by one of the industry’s largest proprietary databases and AI. Our enterprise customers across multiple verticals are empowered to personalize experiences with consumers at an individual level across every channel, delivering better results for marketing programs. Zeta was founded in 2007 by David A. Steinberg and John Sculley and is headquartered in New York City with offices around the world. To learn more, go to <a href="https://www.zetaglobal.com">www.zetaglobal.com</a>.</p><br><p><strong>THE ROLE</strong></p><br><p>Zeta Global is on the cutting edge of integrating large language models (LLMs) into marketing technology. We are seeking a Product Manager to lead our LLM Operations, focusing on agent chaining, model orchestration, and the development of a marketer-friendly agentic tooling. This role is pivotal in making advanced LLM capabilities accessible to marketers in-line and as dedicated product offerings, bridging the gap between technical complexity and user-friendly applications.</p><br><p><strong><span class="EOP SCXW243149147 BCX0"><span class="EOP SCXW238034145 BCX0"><span class="TextRun MacChromeBold SCXW17253922 BCX0" lang="EN-US"><span class="NormalTextRun SCXW17253922 BCX0">RESPONSIBILITIES</span></span><span class="EOP SCXW17253922 BCX0">&nbsp;</span></span></span></strong></p><br><ul><br><li>Agent Chaining and Orchestration: Develop and manage the process of chaining LLM agents for complex workflows, ensuring seamless orchestration and integration within our platform.</li><br><li>Model Workbench Development: Lead the creation of a Model Workbench designed for marketers, making it intuitive for non-technical users to leverage LLM capabilities in their workflows.</li><br><li>MCP Capabilities: Oversee the registration and maintenance of Model Context Protocol server, ensuring that all model capabilities are well-documented and easily accessible.</li><br><li>Agent Evaluation Suites: Build robust evaluation suites for testing and iterating on agent performance, ensuring continuous improvement and reliability.</li><br><li>Context Streaming Services: Implement and manage context streaming services that allow for real-time data flow and contextual awareness in agent interactions.</li><br><li>Technical Troubleshooting and Prototyping: Actively participate in troubleshooting and debugging using tools like LangSmith, and lead by example in building proof-of-concepts (POCs) to demonstrate the feasibility and potential of new ideas.</li><br><li>Advocacy for Quick Iteration: Promote a culture of rapid prototyping and iteration, demonstrating the value of "vibe coding" as a gateway to scalable product development.</li><br></ul><br><p><strong>REQUIREMENTS</strong></p><br><ul><br><li>Experience in Product Management: Demonstrated experience managing complex technical products, particularly those involving LLMs or AI-driven tools.</li><br><li>Technical Proficiency: Strong technical background with hands-on experience in tools like LangSmith, and familiarity with workflow orchestration and LLM agent chaining.</li><br><li>Problem-Solving Skills: Proven ability to troubleshoot, innovate, and rapidly prototype solutions to demonstrate product feasibility.</li><br><li>Communication and Collaboration: Excellent communication skills, with the ability to work closely with cross-functional teams and offshore developers to align on best practices and product goals.</li><br><li>Preferred Qualifications:</li><br><li>Experience with Agentic Infrastructure: Familiarity with integrating traditional ML with generative models to enhance model explainability and user interaction.</li><br><li>Background in Workflow Orchestration: Experience with existing workflow orchestration tools and methodologies.</li><br></ul><br><p><strong>BENEFITS &amp; PERKS</strong></p><br><ul><br><li>Unlimited PTO</li><br><li>Excellent medical, dental, and vision coverage</li><br><li>Employee Equity</li><br><li>Employee Discounts, Virtual Wellness Classes, and Pet Insurance And more!!</li><br></ul><br><p><strong>SALARY RANGE</strong></p><br><p>The salary range for this role is <span><strong>$170,000 - $180,000</strong></span><strong>,</strong> depending on location and experience.&nbsp;</p><br><p><strong>PEOPLE &amp; CULTURE AT ZETA</strong></p><br><p>Zeta considers applicants for employment without regard to, and does not discriminate on the basis of an individual’s sex, race, color, religion, age, disability, status as a veteran, or national or ethnic origin; nor does Zeta discriminate on the basis of sexual orientation, gender identity or expression.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><br><p>We’re committed to building a workplace culture of trust and belonging, so everyone feels invited to bring their whole selves to work. We provide a forum for employees to celebrate, support and advocate for one another. Learn more about our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion here:&nbsp; <a class="fui-Link ___1q1shib f2hkw1w f3rmtva f1ewtqcl fyind8e f1k6fduh f1w7gpdv fk6fouc fjoy568 figsok6 f1s184ao f1mk8lai fnbmjn9 f1o700av f13mvf36 f1cmlufx f9n3di6 f1ids18y f1tx3yz7 f1deo86v f1eh06m1 f1iescvh fhgqx19 f1olyrje f1p93eir f1nev41a f1h8hb77 f1lqvz6u f10aw75t fsle3fq f17ae5zn" href="https://zetaglobal.com/blog/a-look-into-zetas-ergs/">https://zetaglobal.com/blog/a-look-into-zetas-ergs/</a>&nbsp;</p><br><p><strong>ZETA IN THE NEWS!</strong></p><br><p><a class="fui-Link ___1q1shib f2hkw1w f3rmtva f1ewtqcl fyind8e f1k6fduh f1w7gpdv fk6fouc fjoy568 figsok6 f1s184ao f1mk8lai fnbmjn9 f1o700av f13mvf36 f1cmlufx f9n3di6 f1ids18y f1tx3yz7 f1deo86v f1eh06m1 f1iescvh fhgqx19 f1olyrje f1p93eir f1nev41a f1h8hb77 f1lqvz6u f10aw75t fsle3fq f17ae5zn" href="https://zetaglobal.com/press/?cat=press-releases">https://zetaglobal.com/press/?cat=press-releases</a>&nbsp;</p><br></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/zeta-global-senior-product-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/zeta-global-senior-product-manager</a></p>
</description>

    <item>
      <title>Riverside.fm: DevOps Engineer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Tel Aviv-Yafo, Tel Aviv District, Israel
</p>

<p>For many of us there’s that one podcast we never miss, and video content is part of our daily routine, whether it’s professional or personal. But how many of us truly understand the effort that goes on behind the scenes? Here at Riverside, we know it well. That’s exactly why we built an AI-powered platform that helps content creators, podcasters, marketeers, and more at major brands like Netflix, Disney, Google, and Microsoft to create high-quality content with ease.</p><p><br></p><p>Riverside’s technology streamlines the entire content creation process, turning ideas into professional-grade content with the highest production standards, without requiring expensive equipment or external services. The secret? AI-driven tools that replace traditional production roles like editing, directing, and design, automating the entire process at the click of a button.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>About the Engineering Team</strong></p><p>We’re a team of smart, curious engineers building scalable, reliable systems that power content creation for millions. We work with modern web technologies, tackle real-world challenges in distributed systems, and keep things practical - no overengineering, just solid solutions. If you love solving tough problems, moving fast, and building tech that creators actually use, you’ll fit right in.</p><p><strong>On your day-to-day</strong></p><p>We’re hiring a&nbsp;<strong>Mid-Level DevOps Engineer</strong>&nbsp;to join our growing infrastructure team. You'll be working hands-on with modern AWS-native technologies, helping us scale secure and observable cloud environments, while partnering closely with developers to build and ship reliably. </p><p><strong>Responsibilities</strong></p><ul><li>Manage scalable, production-grade<strong> AWS</strong> infrastructure with <strong>Terraform</strong></li><li>Maintain Kubernetes clusters, including deployments, autoscaling, and ingress</li><li>Handle cross-service communication using&nbsp;<strong>Kafka</strong>,&nbsp;<strong>SQS</strong>, or&nbsp;<strong>PubSub</strong></li><li>Set up observability and alerting using Grafana, Prometheus, and log aggregation tools</li><li>Integrate Cloudflare for traffic protection, rate limiting, and caching</li><li>Manage and improve CI/CD workflows using&nbsp;<strong>GitHub Actions</strong>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<strong>ArgoCD</strong></li><li>Collaborate directly with developers on environment setup, debug sessions, and deployments</li><li>Implement IaC and GitOps patterns across services and teams</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>﻿</strong></p> <p><strong>What Will Make You Stand Out?</strong></p><ul><li>3+ years in DevOps or Platform Engineering</li><li>Deep knowledge of AWS networking, IAM, ALB/NLB, and VPC routing</li><li>Strong Terraform experience and infrastructure modularization</li><li>Kubernetes hands-on experience including Helm, scaling, and security contexts</li><li>Comfortable with observability tooling and alerting practices</li><li>Good communication skills; experience supporting developers day-to-day</li></ul><p><strong>Nice to Have</strong></p><ul><li>Exposure to&nbsp;<strong>Crossplane</strong>,&nbsp;<strong>service mesh</strong>&nbsp;(Istio), or&nbsp;<strong>Temporal</strong></li><li>Familiarity with working in developer-facing platform teams</li><li>Experience setting up internal developer platforms or self-service infra</li></ul><p><br></p><p><strong>Bottom line?</strong> If you wanna take part in transforming how people and businesses share their stories globally, Riverside’s your place. The work is challenging, the culture is fast-paced, and the people are exceptionally brilliant. And if that’s not enough, we guarantee that your ideas will genuinely make an impact.</p><p><br></p>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/riverside-fm-devops-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/riverside-fm-devops-engineer</a></p>
</description>
