      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Cambridge, Cambridgeshire
</p>

<div class="job-details_jobDescription__1dErB job-details_jobSection__1snwj"><div> <p><strong>Job Title:</strong> Senior DevOps Engineer (Contract)</p> <p><strong>Location:</strong> Remote</p> <p><strong>Contract:</strong> 3 Months (possible extension)</p> <p>We are seeking a highly skilled Senior DevOps Engineer to join on an initial 3-month contract, with potential for extension. This is a fully remote role, ideal for an experienced contractor who can hit the ground running and deliver immediate value.</p> <p>You'll play a key role in supporting and enhancing the company's AWS cloud infrastructure, driving automation, and ensuring robust, scalable solutions are delivered effectively.</p> <p><strong>Key Responsibilities</strong></p> <ul> <li>Design, build, and maintain scalable AWS cloud environments.</li><li>Develop and manage Infrastructure as Code using Terraform.</li><li>Automate deployment pipelines, monitoring, and operational tasks.</li><li>Use Python scripting to create and optimise tools, processes, and integrations.</li><li>Troubleshoot and resolve issues across cloud infrastructure and CI/CD workflows.</li><li>Collaborate with development and operations teams to ensure smooth delivery.</li></ul> <p><strong>Required Skills &amp; Experience</strong></p> <ul> <li>Proven experience as a Senior DevOps Engineer, 5+ years </li><li>Strong hands-on expertise with AWS Cloud Computing.</li><li>Advanced knowledge of Terraform for IaC.</li><li>Proficiency in Python scripting for automation and tooling.</li><li>Solid understanding of CI/CD pipelines and DevOps best practices.</li><li>Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to work independently.</li></ul> </div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/cooper-lomaz-recruitment-senior-devops-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/cooper-lomaz-recruitment-senior-devops-engineer</a></p>

    <item>
      <title>Coupa Software: Sr. Product Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Ann Arbor, Michigan, United States
</p>

<div class="content"><div class="section-wrapper page-full-width"><div class="section page-centered"><div>Coupa makes margins multiply through its community-generated AI and industry-leading total spend management platform for businesses large and small. Coupa AI is informed by trillions of dollars of direct and indirect spend data across a global network of 10M+ buyers and suppliers. We empower you with the ability to predict, prescribe, and automate smarter, more profitable business decisions to improve operating margins.</div><div><br></div><div><b>Why join Coupa?</b></div><div><br></div><div> Pioneering Technology: At Coupa, we're at the forefront of innovation, leveraging the latest technology to empower our customers with greater efficiency and visibility in their spend.</div><div> Collaborative Culture: We value collaboration and teamwork, and our culture is driven by transparency, openness, and a shared commitment to excellence.</div><div> Global Impact: Join a company where your work has a global, measurable impact on our clients, the business, and each other.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Learn more on <a href="https://insde.co/uzoy">Life at Coupa</a> blog and hear from our employees about their experiences working at Coupa.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><b>The Impact of a Sr. Product Manager at Coupa:</b></div><div><br></div><div>We are seeking an experienced Senior Product Manager to lead the strategy, execution, and evolution of our Clarity Design System while driving AI-powered capabilities that shape the future of our product experiences. This role sits at the intersection of design, technology, and innovation, enabling consistency, scalability, and intelligence across our platform.</div><div>You’ll partner closely with design, engineering, and AI teams to ensure our design system becomes a robust foundation for delivering delightful, accessible, and efficient user experiences—while also integrating cutting-edge AI components to support our broader AI strategy.</div><div><br></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>What You'll Do: </h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>Own the vision and roadmap for our Design System, ensuring it aligns with product and brand strategy.</li><li>Define and deliver AI-driven enhancements within the design system, such as adaptive components, personalization, and intelligent UI patterns.</li><li>Partner with cross-functional teams (Design, Engineering, AI, Product) to implement scalable design solutions across multiple products and platforms.</li><li>Establish governance frameworks, best practices, and contribution models for the Design System.</li><li>Drive adoption and evangelization of the Design System across internal teams and external partners.</li><li>Collaborate with AI product leaders to identify opportunities for integrating AI-driven capabilities in core components and workflows.</li><li>Monitor emerging trends in design systems, AI, and UX to ensure we remain at the forefront of innovation.</li><li>Define and track success metrics for both design system adoption and AI integration impact.</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>What You Will Bring to Coupa: </h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>5+ years of product management experience, including experience with design systems or design tooling at scale.</li><li>Strong understanding of UX principles, design tokens, component libraries, and accessibility standards.</li><li>Experience working with AI/ML technologies and understanding their application in design or product experiences.</li><li>Proven ability to manage complex roadmaps, prioritize competing demands, and deliver outcomes at scale.</li><li>Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills—able to influence across design, engineering, and leadership teams.</li><li>Familiarity with front-end frameworks (React, Angular, etc.), design tools (Figma)</li><li>Background in component-driven development and design-to-code workflows.</li><li>Passion for creating consistent, beautiful, and intelligent user experiences.</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div>#LI-AA2</div><div><br></div><div><span>#LI-Remote</span></div><div><br></div><div>Coupa complies with relevant laws and regulations regarding equal opportunity and offers a welcoming and inclusive work environment. Decisions related to hiring, compensation, training, or evaluating performance are made fairly, and we provide equal employment opportunities to all qualified candidates and employees.&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div>Please be advised that inquiries or resumes from recruiters will not be accepted.</div><div><br></div><div>By submitting your application, you acknowledge that you have read <a href="https://www.coupa.com/privacy-policy">Coupa’s Privacy Policy</a> and understand that Coupa receives/collects your application, including your personal data, for the purposes of managing Coupa's ongoing recruitment and placement activities, including for employment purposes in the event of a successful application and for notification of future job opportunities if you did not succeed the first time. You will find more details about how your application is processed, the purposes of processing, and how long we retain your application in our Privacy Policy.</div></div></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/coupa-software-sr-product-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/coupa-software-sr-product-manager</a></p>

      <title>Mactores: Full Stack Developer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Mumbai, MH
</p>

<div class="content"><div class="section-wrapper page-full-width"><div class="section page-centered"><div>Mactores is a trusted leader among businesses in providing modern data platform solutions. Since 2008, Mactores have been enabling businesses to accelerate their value through automation by providing End-to-End Data Solutions that are automated, agile, and secure. We collaborate with customers to strategize, navigate, and accelerate an ideal path forward with a digital transformation via assessments, migration, or modernization.</div><div><br></div><div><span>We are seeking a passionate and detail-oriented Full Stack Developer (Associate) with 2+ years of experience to join our team. In this role, you will build and maintain marketing web pages, develop interactive demos using cutting-edge AI tools (such as v0 and Lovable), and contribute to both front-end and back-end infrastructure. This is a hands-on role where you’ll code daily, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and help shape engaging digital experiences for our customers.</span></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>What you will do</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>Build and maintain marketing pages and interactive demos leveraging AI tools (e.g., v0, Lovable).</li><li>Develop high-quality, testable, and efficient code that can scale to serve a significant number of users.</li><li>Architect, implement, and drive improvements to front-end and back-end infrastructure.</li><li>Ensure proper implementation of security and data protection measures.</li><li>Follow full lifecycle software development practices, including design, coding, testing, deployment, and maintenance.</li><li>Collaborate with design and marketing teams to deliver seamless, responsive user experiences.</li><li>Identify opportunities to enhance user engagement through innovative tech solutions.</li><li>Stay updated on emerging trends in web development and inform senior leadership of new tech ideas, products, and approaches that could improve business growth and customer experiences.</li><li>Adhere to cloud application development practices and create high-quality, testable, and efficient code that can scale with a significant number of users.</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>What you need for this opportunity</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related field.</li><li>2+ years of hands-on experience in full-stack development.</li><li>Proficiency with MongoDB, Express.js, React.js, Node.js, HTML, and CSS.</li><li>Strong understanding of RESTful APIs and service security best practices.</li><li>Familiarity with GitHub and collaborative coding practices.</li><li>Experience in building highly scalable, data-intensive products.</li><li>Proficiency with OOP languages and solid problem-solving skills.</li><li>Knowledge of responsive design techniques and mobile-first development.</li><li>Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work in a fast-paced startup environment.</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>You are preferred if you have</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>Experience/ Knowledge working with Ecommerce portals.</li><li>Unit Testing frameworks and tools (Karma, Mocha, Jasmine, Jest, etc)</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><b>Life at Mactores</b></div><div><br></div><div>We care about creating a culture that makes a real difference in the lives of every Mactorian. Our 10 Core Leadership Principles that honor Decision-making, Leadership, Collaboration, and Curiosity drive how we work.</div><div><br></div><div>1. Be one step ahead</div><div>2. Deliver the best</div><div>3. Be bold</div><div>4. Pay attention to the detail</div><div>5. Enjoy the challenge</div><div>6. Be curious and take action</div><div>7. Take leadership</div><div>8. Own it</div><div>9. Deliver value</div><div>10. Be collaborative</div><div><br></div><div>We would like you to read more details about the work culture on <a class="postings-link" href="https://mactores.com/careers">https://mactores.com/careers</a>&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><b><span>The Path to Joining the Mactores Team</span></b></div><div>At Mactores, our recruitment process is structured around three distinct stages:</div><div><br></div><div><b>Pre-Employment Assessment:</b>&nbsp;</div><div>You will be invited to participate in a series of pre-employment evaluations to assess your technical proficiency and suitability for the role.</div><div><br></div><div><b>Managerial Interview: </b>The hiring manager will engage with you in multiple discussions, lasting anywhere from 30 minutes to an hour, to assess your technical skills, hands-on experience, leadership potential, and communication abilities.</div><div><br></div><div><b>HR Discussion: </b>During this 30-minute session, you'll have the opportunity to discuss the offer and next steps with a member of the HR team.</div><div><br></div><div>At Mactores, we are committed to providing equal opportunities in all of our employment practices, and we do not discriminate based on race, religion, gender, national origin, age, disability, marital status, military status, genetic information, or any other category protected by federal, state, and local laws. This policy extends to all aspects of the employment relationship, including recruitment, compensation, promotions, transfers, disciplinary action, layoff, training, and social and recreational programs. All employment decisions will be made in compliance with these principles.</div><div><br></div><div><br></div><div><b>Note: </b>Please answer as many questions as possible with this application to accelerate the hiring process.  </div></div></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mactores-full-stack-developer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mactores-full-stack-developer</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Zocks: Senior Product Marketing Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Remote US
</p>

<div class="content"><div class="section-wrapper page-full-width"><div class="section page-centered"><div><span>About Zocks</span></div><div><span>Zocks is the AI assistant for financial advisors. Our platform saves advisors 10 hours a week by automating their meeting notes, intake forms, client emails and more—so they can focus on what matters most: their clients. We’re backed by top investors, used by thousands of advisors, and growing fast. Now we’re looking for a Senior Product Marketing Manager to help us go even </span><a class="postings-link" href="http://faster.The"><span>faster.</span></a></div><div><br></div><div><span>The</span><span> Role</span></div><div><span>As our first Senior Product Marketing Manager, you’ll take the lead on how we position, package, and launch our product. You’ll work closely with marketing, product, sales, and customer success to turn product capabilities into compelling stories that drive adoption, conversion, and growth. You’ll own everything from messaging frameworks and launch plans to sales enablement and competitor intel. This is a strategic and hands-on role—perfect for someone who knows how to dig into the product, talk to users, and ship clear, high-impact work fast.</span></div><div><br></div><div><span>What </span><span>You’ll Do</span></div><div><span>Develop product positioning and messaging that’s clear, compelling, and differentiated</span></div><div><span>Own and execute product launches—from internal readiness to external campaigns</span></div><div><span>Create and maintain sales enablement materials (decks, one-pagers, FAQs, etc.)</span></div><div><span>Build and evolve messaging frameworks for features, use cases, and personas</span></div><div><span>Partner with product to influence roadmap and bring new features to market</span></div><div><span>Collaborate with marketing to develop high-performing content and campaigns</span></div><div><span>Conduct competitive research and market analysis to guide positioningTalk to customers regularly to understand their needs and language</span></div><div><br></div><div><span>What We’re Looking For</span></div><div><span>6+ years in product marketing, ideally at a B2B SaaS company</span></div><div><span>Experience owning product positioning, messaging, and launches</span></div><div><span>Strong writing skills—you can translate technical features into clear, benefit-driven messaging</span></div><div><span>Proven ability to work cross-functionally and lead projects from strategy to execution</span></div><div><span>Comfortable interviewing users and using insights to shape messaging</span></div><div><span>Scrappy and adaptable—you’re excited to build, test, and iterate quickly</span></div><div><span>Bonus: experience in financial services, advisor tech, or AI-powered products</span></div><div><br></div><div><span>Why Zocks</span></div><div><span>Category-defining product with strong market pull and real user love</span></div><div><span>High-impact role with ownership and autonomy from day one</span></div><div><span>Competitive compensation and equity</span></div><div><span>Remote-first culture with flexible hours</span></div><div><span>Health, dental, and vision benefits</span></div><div><br></div><div><b><span>Want to help define the voice of the most useful AI in financial services?</span></b></div><div><b><span>Apply now and let’s build something great together.</span></b></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div>$170,000 - $190,000 a year</div></div></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/zocks-senior-product-marketing-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/zocks-senior-product-marketing-manager</a></p>
</description>

      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>

      <state></state>

      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> City of London, London
</p>

<div class="job-details_jobDescription__1dErB job-details_jobSection__1snwj"><div><strong>Full Stack Developer – £425-475pd PAYE – 12 month contract – Remote </strong> <br><br><strong>Overview:</strong><br>I’m currently partnered with an innovative global technology company who are seeking an experienced Full Stack Developer to join their dynamic tech team. In this role, you’ll be responsible for developing robust, scalable applications from front-end interfaces  to back-end systems, contributing to every stage of the software development lifecycle.<br><br>This is a hands-on development role that requires someone who can work across the entire tech stack from crafting seamless user experiences in ReactJS, to writing robust backend logic in PHP/Hack, and developing automation tools and data pipelines with Python.  You’ll collaborate closely with cross-functional teams, bring technical ideas to life, and contribute to the full software development lifecycle from architecture and coding to testing and deployment.<br><br><strong>Role &amp; Responsibilities:</strong><br><ul> <li>Build and maintain full stack applications using ReactJS, PHP/Hack, and Python</li><li>Design user interfaces and implement server-side logic and databases for optimal performance</li><li>Create Python-based solutions (e.g., automation libraries, data processing pipelines)</li><li>Analyse complex problems and deliver effective technical solutions</li><li>Write clean, efficient, and well-documented code</li><li>Support system performance monitoring, incident resolution, and disaster recovery planning</li><li>Evaluate new project requirements and estimate feasibility, cost, and timelines</li></ul> <br><strong>Essential Skills &amp; Experience: </strong><br><ul> <li>7–10 years of proven full stack development experience</li><li>Strong command of ReactJS, PHP/Hack, and Python</li><li>Solid understanding of front-end and back-end architecture</li><li>Hands-on experience with full lifecycle application development</li><li>Excellent debugging, problem-solving, and analytical skills</li><li>Strong verbal and written communication skills</li><li>Familiarity with SQL, VisualBasic, and similar dev tools is a plus</li><li>Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field (required)</li><li>Master’s degree preferred</li></ul> <br><strong>Package: </strong><br><ul> <li>Day rate - £425-475pd PAYE</li><li>15% bonus</li><li>Fully remote role</li><li>12-month contract (possibility to extend)</li></ul> </div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/intec-select-limited-full-stack-developer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/intec-select-limited-full-stack-developer</a></p>
</description>

    <item>
      <title>Xero: Senior Product Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Wellington, NZ / Auckland, NZ / Remote, NZ / Hawkes Bay, NZ
</p>

<div class="content"><div class="section-wrapper page-full-width"><div class="section page-centered"><div><b>Our Purpose&nbsp;</b></div><div><span>At Xero, we’re here to help you supercharge your business. We do this by automating routine tasks, surfacing actionable insights and connecting businesses with the right data, advisors and apps. When that happens, we’re not only making life better for small business, we’ll be building a stronger economy that can change the world.</span></div><div><br></div><div><b>Senior Product Manager - Payments</b></div><div><span>As a</span><b><span> Senior Product Manager,</span></b><span> you will be responsible for excellence in driving customer and business value through ownership of new and existing products. You will be comfortable working in complex product spaces that require navigating larger degrees of ambiguity and comprehensive stakeholder management. You will manage products end-to-end, from early-stage research and ideation, through to implementation and improvement over time. Your impact will be measured by key commercial and customer success metrics.</span></div><div><br></div><div><b>About the team:</b></div><div><span>Within our Payments Portfolio, you’ll be joining one of&nbsp; our Get Paid teams to help us deliver the best payment outcomes for our customers and drive Xero’s payments revenue ambitions. This is an exciting time to join the team as we embark on year 1 of a 3 years strategy to transform our payments business.&nbsp;&nbsp;</span></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>What we're looking for:</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>5+ years of product management experience, including improving customer workflows and learning through experimentation.</li><li>A proven track record of delivering products that drive significant customer and business impact.</li><li>Strong commercial acumen. In payments, we carry financial targets so we need someone that can translate the work into financial outcomes and deliver results.</li><li>Can get technically deep and engage in detailed engineering design opportunities.</li><li>Can execute an API-first strategy and support engineering efficiency goals.</li><li>Have a strong interest in AI and how it can improve outcomes for customers and Xeros.</li><li>Strong analytical skills, with the ability to interpret complex data and translate insights into actionable strategies.</li><li>Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills, comfort with presenting to senior leadership.</li><li>A growth mindset, with a passion for learning, innovation, and pushing boundaries.</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>What you'll do:</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li><b>Strategise Product Vision:</b> Lead the strategic framing of products within Xero's overall strategy and future objectives.</li><li><b>Drive Customer-Centricity: </b>Lead customer engagement, research, and insight collection to embed customer data in decision-making and risk assessment.</li><li><b>Problem Identification &amp; Solution Framing:</b> Identify valuable customer problems and comprehensively frame solutions for testing and refinement, even in complex environments.</li><li><b>Product Line Strategy &amp; Resourcing:</b> Develop and secure funding and resources for the product line strategy.</li><li><b>Oversee Product Delivery &amp; Launch:</b> Lead the development of product roadmaps, release strategies, and ensure effective product launches.</li><li><b>Manage Product Risks: </b>Take responsibility for managing non-functional risks (security, privacy, compliance, etc.) and lead trade-off decisions.</li><li><b>Monitor &amp; Optimise Performance: </b>Consistently review, monitor, and analyze commercial and customer results, leading iterative improvements to existing products.</li><li><b>Guide End-to-End Product Lifecycle:</b> Manage products across their entire lifecycle, from launch to sunset, ensuring continuity and strategic alignment.</li><li><b>Develop Product Practice &amp; Team:</b> Contribute to developing and implementing product practices, and train, coach, and recruit team members.</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><span><b>Why Xero?&nbsp;</b></span></div><div>Offering very generous paid leave to use however you’d like (plus statutory holidays!), dedicated paid leave to care for your physical and mental wellbeing as well as an Employee Assistance Program to access mental health care for you and your family, free medical insurance, wellbeing and sports programmes, employee resource groups, 26 weeks of paid parental leave for primary caregivers, an Employee Share Plan, beautiful offices, flexible working, career development, and many other benefits that reflect our human value, you’ll do the best work of your life at Xero.</div></div></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/xero-senior-product-manager-1">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/xero-senior-product-manager-1</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Fun.xyz: Technical Product Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> New York, NY
</p>

<div class="content"><div class="section-wrapper page-full-width"><div class="section page-centered"><div><b>About us</b></div><div><br></div><div>At&nbsp;<a class="postings-link" href="http://fun.xyz/">Fun.xyz</a>, we are pioneering the future of decentralized technology, creating groundbreaking blockchain solutions and web3 applications that engage and empower users worldwide.</div><div>Our mission is to simplify and supercharge the way people interact with blockchain technology, building products that are as intuitive as they are powerful. From seamless payment solutions to innovative dApps, we’re focused on creating tools that enable users and businesses to unlock the full potential of decentralization.</div><div>We’re a dynamic team of engineers, designers, and innovators driven by a shared passion for crypto and blockchain technology. At&nbsp;<a class="postings-link" href="http://fun.xyz/">Fun.xyz</a>, we don’t just build products — we’re shaping the future of the web.</div><div><br></div><div><b>About the role</b></div><div><br></div><div>As a Technical Product Manager at&nbsp;<a class="postings-link" href="http://fun.xyz/">Fun.xyz</a>, you will be at the forefront of building cutting-edge blockchain products that redefine user experiences in the decentralized web. You'll collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver seamless, scalable, and secure solutions, bridging the gap between technical complexity and user-friendly design.</div><div>This role offers a unique opportunity to work on highly impactful projects, including the development of the highest-converting crypto checkout in the industry. You will shape product strategies, drive execution, and ensure that our solutions empower users and businesses alike.</div><div>If you're a results-driven leader with a deep understanding of blockchain technology and an entrepreneurial mindset, this role is your chance to make a tangible impact on the evolution of web3.</div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>Qualifications</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>You have a Bachelor's degree in Computer Sciences, engineering or a related field</li><li>7+ years in Product management and strategy, bonus if a few of those years being in Crypto</li><li>Crypto-forward experience, including familiarity with onchain activity such as interacting with Ethereum addresses, using ENS, and engaging with dApps or blockchain-based services</li><li>Consistent track record of leading ambitious and ambiguous projects</li><li>Obsession with product quality and an eye for design</li><li>Experience in highly-cross functional environments</li><li>Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making skills</li><li>Strong written and verbal communication skills</li><li>Technical experience (Previously a SWE, Protocol engineer, Data analyst, etc.)</li><li>An entrepreneurial mindset with the ability to succeed in a fast-paced environment</li><div><br></div><div><b>Responsibilities</b></div><div><br></div><li>Help&nbsp;<a class="postings-link" href="http://fun.xyz/">Fun.xyz</a>&nbsp;build out the highest converting Checkout product in the industry</li><li>Define the strategy and roadmap for engineering, attaining maximum alignment with business values and goals</li><li>Deeply understand <a class="postings-link" href="http://Fun.xyz">Fun.xyz</a>’s diverse client and user base, and their nuanced business models</li><li>Own all facets of a product area from crafting the vision and strategy to executing</li><li>Dive into technologies spanning a mix of off-chain &amp; completely on-chain protocols</li><li>Partner closely with engineering, design, data, customer support, legal, and operations teams</li><li>Make trade-offs across multiple complex dimensions</li><li>Leverage data and analytics to inform strategy and measure success</li><li>Make multitudes of micro-decisions everyday that will impact the business</li><div><br></div></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><b>What sets you apart</b></div><div><br></div><div>You are extremely organized</div><div>You are hyper detail-oriented</div><div>You take pride and set a high bar for your work, and will not hesitate to hold others to the same bar</div><div>You have demonstrated experience influencing stakeholders (partners, executives, engineering, customers) in order to achieve results</div><div>You have mentored other product managers and/or engineers in the past</div><div>You are a big Do-er and base your work around execution</div><div>You are not afraid to roll up your sleeves and dive into the trenches with the team, be it coding, talking to customers, plumbing data, writing documentation, preparing decks, etc.</div><div>You have a track record of excellence (from school, hobbies, side projects, etc)</div></div></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/fun-xyz-technical-product-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/fun-xyz-technical-product-manager</a></p>
</description>

    <item>
      <title>Tenth Revolution Group: Product Manager - fully remote</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> London
</p>

<div class="job-details_jobDescription__1dErB job-details_jobSection__1snwj"><div> <p>A software company which provides a data routing and optimisation platform are looking to recruit a Product Manager to join their rapidly growing scale-up. As a Product Manager you'll play a pivotal role in shaping the future of the platform, working at  the intersection of customer needs, business strategy and technology. You will lead discovery efforts to identify opportunities, deliver meaningful solutions to market and continuously measure success to drive improvement and growth. You'll be responsible  for informing and executing product strategy and roadmap, championing the voice of the customer and collaborating across engineering, sales, marketing and customer success to ensure successful outcomes.</p> <p><strong>Key Responsibilities </strong></p> <ul> <li>Assist in the definition and communication of product vision, strategy and roadmap</li><li>Lead discovery work by conducting weekly customer interviews and synthesising insights into actionable deliverables</li><li>Translate problems into outcomes, shaping product opportunities and prioritising for impact</li><li>Own delivery from definition through to go-to-market - write clear specs, validate solutions with users and partner closely with engineering</li><li>Measure the impact of product releases against defined success metrics and iterate to achieve the desired results</li><li>Collaborate cross-functionally with Sales, Customer Success, Marketing and Engineering to deliver valuable product experiences</li><li>Work closely with data and market research to inform prioritisation and roadmap decisions</li><li>Define, track, and report on KPIs</li><li>Communicate progress, outcomes and strategy to internal and external stakeholders regularly</li><li>Foster a customer-centric culture by championing feedback loops from customers, internal users and the market.</li></ul> <p>The successful candidate will have 5 years' experience as a Product Manager in a fast-paced B2B SaaS environment, ideally within marketing tech or data platforms, working as a sole Product Manager or a dedicated Product Manager to a small development team.  They are looking for someone with a track record of shipping successful products and owning the full product lifecycle, as well as strong experience working in cross-functional teams including engineers, designers, data analysts and GTM teams.</p> </div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/tenth-revolution-group-product-manager-fully-remote">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/tenth-revolution-group-product-manager-fully-remote</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Exact Sourcing Ltd: Customer Support Specialist</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Cambridge, Cambridgeshire
</p>

<div class="job-details_jobDescription__1dErB job-details_jobSection__1snwj"><div> <p>Our client a fast-growing company providing a Saas solution for Accounting Teams. They are looking for a Customer Support Specialist with an accounting background to help the users of their software. You will handle support tickets create help content and  work with tools like Gainsight PX to support customers. It may be you are working in accounts at the moment, with an interest or experience in supporting customers, and an interest in Technology solutions.</p> <p><strong>Key Responsibilities for the Customer Support Specialist:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Respond to customer inquiries via chat, email, and ticketing systems</li><li>Troubleshoot and escalate product issues</li><li>Create and update help articles and tutorials</li><li>Analyse user behaviour and support Customer Success using Gainsight</li><li>Collect feedback to help improve the product and user experience</li></ul> <p><strong>Requirements for the Customer Support Specialist:</strong></p> <ul> <li>2+ years in customer support, customer success, or educational content</li><li>Strong communication, problem-solving, and organizational skills</li><li>Experience with Microsoft 365/SharePoint</li><li>Accounting background or experience with accounting software/workpapers</li><li>Bonus: SaaS experience, content performance tracking, or UX knowledge</li></ul> <p><strong>What We Offer for the Customer Support Specialist :</strong></p> <ul> <li>Competitive pay and benefits</li><li>Fully remote work</li><li>A chance to shape customer experience and content strategy</li><li>Supportive, collaborative team making an impact in the accounting industry</li></ul> <p>By applying for this Customer Support Specialist role, you are agreeing to your CV being held on our database, stored confidentially and securely. Your CV details will be used and kept only to provide recruitment services from us. We will only send your  details to clients once discussed with you and your data will not be shared with any other third party. You can ask for the deletion or correction of your data, or to opt out of this service at any point by informing Exact Sourcing.</p> <p>We very much look forward to hearing from you and will be reviewing all applications over the next 2 to 3 weeks. If you are selected, we will be in contact and if not, we will be keeping your details on file for any future vacancies.</p> <p>Exact Sourcing is an equal opportunities employer.</p> </div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/exact-sourcing-ltd-customer-support-specialist">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/exact-sourcing-ltd-customer-support-specialist</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Deepset: Product Marketing Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Berlin, Germany, London, UK, Barcelona, Spain
</p>

<div class="description-wrapper description-wrapper-shown" lang="en"><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">TL;DR</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br>                        We’re hiring a <strong>Product Marketing Manager</strong> to drive go-to-market, sales enablement, and content for our LLM-powered platform. You’ll collaborate across Product, Sales, and Marketing to shape messaging, launch new features, support campaigns, and turn market insights into action. Ideal for someone who blends strategy, storytelling, and technical curiosity.<br>                    </div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">Why deepset</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br>                            At deepset, we’re on a mission to make custom AI solutions accessible to every organization. With Haystack, thousands of developers build advanced LLM applications every day, while our enterprise-ready AI Platform helps companies turn large language models into business value. We’re remote-first, flexible, and built on a culture of trust and ownership. You’ll collaborate with top-tier tech talent, tackle meaningful challenges, and help transform complex AI into solutions that are simple, powerful, and ready for the real world.<br>                        </div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">Requirements</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br><ul><li>3+ years in B2B SaaS product marketing, ideally with AI or developer tools</li><li>Excellent communication skills with the ability to simplify complex topics</li><li>Proven experience building sales materials, content, and messaging frameworks</li><li>Strong collaboration and stakeholder alignment skills</li><li>Confident in market research, competitor analysis, and customer insights</li><li>Comfortable working cross-functionally in a fast-paced environment</li><li>Analytical mindset with a strategic bias for action</li></ul><br></div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">Nice to have</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br><ul><li>Experience marketing technical products to both business and developer audiences</li><li>Familiarity with LLMs, NLP, or open-source developer tools</li><li>Background supporting enterprise go-to-market teams</li><li>Hands-on content creation skills (blog, video, social, enablement)</li></ul><br></div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">Benefits</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br><ul><li><span>&nbsp;</span>Remote-first setup with flexible hours &amp; tech of your choice<span>&nbsp;</span></li><li>30 days vacation + extra days for family sick leave</li><li>Competitive salary &amp; stock options for every team member</li><li>Monthly sports &amp; mental health support allowance with Oliva</li><li>Annual learning &amp; development budget</li><li>Monthly team socials &amp; in-person meetups</li><li>Dog-friendly Berlin HQ</li></ul><br></div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">About us</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br>                        Founded in 2018, deepset builds open and enterprise-grade tools that help teams turn language into action. From Haystack, our open-source framework, to the deepset AI Platform, we give developers and organizations the building blocks to solve real problems with LLMs. Backed by GV and Balderton, we’re trusted by startups and global enterprises alike.<br><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><span><br><strong>Visit us to learn more:</strong> <a href="https://www.deepset.ai/">Website</a> | <a href="https://github.com/deepset-ai/haystack">GitHub&nbsp;</a>| <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/company/deepset-ai">Linkedin</a> | <a href="https://twitter.com/deepset_ai">X (Twitter)</a></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span><br></div><br><br></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/deepset-product-marketing-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/deepset-product-marketing-manager</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Interop Labs: DevOps Engineer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Remote
</p>

<div class="description-wrapper description-wrapper-shown" lang="en"><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">About the role</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br>                        As a DevOps Engineer at Interop Labs, you’ll play a key role in ensuring the reliability, scalability, and performance of our infrastructure. You’ll work closely with Engineers to build and maintain CI/CD pipelines, automate systems, and support development and production environments across multiple blockchain ecosystems. This is a hands-on role for someone who thrives in a fast-paced, distributed environment and is passionate about automation, monitoring, and operational excellence.<br>                    </div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">Your responsibilities</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br><ul><li><p>Build, maintain, and optimize CI/CD pipelines to ensure fast, reliable deployments across multiple environments.</p></li><li><p>Automate infrastructure provisioning and configuration using tools like Terraform and scripting languages.</p></li><li><p>Improve monitoring, alerting, and observability using tools such as Grafana, Prometheus, ELK, etc.</p></li><li><p>Support and scale containerized infrastructure using Kubernetes, Docker, and cloud services (e.g., AWS).</p></li><li><p>Troubleshoot infrastructure issues across the stack, identifying root causes and implementing solutions.</p></li><li><p>Collaborate with engineering teams to streamline development workflows and ensure smooth production operations.</p></li><li><p>Contribute to initiatives that enhance infrastructure reliability, security, and performance.</p></li><li><p>Stay current with emerging DevOps and Web3 technologies and recommend improvements to our systems.</p></li></ul><br></div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">Your profile</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br><ul><li><p>3+ years of experience in DevOps, SRE, or infrastructure engineering roles.</p></li><li><p>Strong hands-on experience with Kubernetes, Docker, and at least one major cloud platform (AWS, GCP, or similar).</p></li><li><p>Proficiency in scripting languages and infrastructure as code (e.g., Bash, Python, Terraform).</p></li><li><p>Familiarity with CI/CD tools (e.g., GitHub Actions, CircleCI, Jenkins) and deployment automation.</p></li><li><p>Experience with observability and monitoring tools like Grafana, Prometheus, or ELK.</p></li><li><p>Understanding of blockchain infrastructure, DeFi protocols, or decentralized systems.</p></li><li><p>A proactive, collaborative, and problem-solving mindset with the ability to work independently in a remote team.</p></li></ul><br></div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">Why us?</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br><ul><li><p>Autonomous, distributed environment with the opportunity to work collaboratively in a diverse team worldwide.</p></li><li><p>The scope to contribute to high-impact work and make a difference in a decentralized protocol.</p></li><li><p>The chance to challenge yourself whilst learning heaps of stuff in the process.</p></li><li><p>Unlimited time off throughout the year to rest and recharge.</p></li><li><p>Competitive compensation with stock options, experiencing growth from the initial phase.</p></li></ul><br></div><br><br><br><h5 class="detail-block-title">About us</h5><br><div class="detail-block-description"><br><span><strong>Interop Labs builds interoperability technology.</strong></span><span>&nbsp;&nbsp;<br>&nbsp;&nbsp;<br></span><span>Interoperability between blockchains is crucial technology infrastructure for the growth of Web3 and the advancement of internet technology as a whole. Interop Labs is the initial developer of <strong>Axelar Network</strong> - the programmable Web3 interoperability platform, scaling the next generation of internet applications to billions of users. Axelar network's key attributes are programmability, security and scalability.&nbsp;</span><br><br><span>Axelar (<a href="https://axelar.network/">https://axelar.network</a>) has raised over $100 million from leading VCs including DCVC, Galaxy, Polychain, Dragonfly, Coinbase, and more. Its diverse partner ecosystem spans industry heavyweights such as Ripple, Circle, dYdX, Uniswap, JPMorgan, Deutsche Bank, Microsoft, and others—underscoring Axelar’s unique market position and opportunity in unifying stacks between traditional finance and decentralized ecosystems. Axelar protocol was founded by Sergey Gorbunov (MIT PhD, UWaterloo Professor) and Georgios Vlachos (MIT MSc, Math Gold Medalist), who previously helped to build and launch Algorand.&nbsp;</span><br><br><span><em>Interop Labs is committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, or any legally protected status.<br></em><br></span><br></div><br><br></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/interop-labs-devops-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/interop-labs-devops-engineer</a></p>
</description>

      <title>Kinsta: Product Marketing Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Toronto
</p>

<div class="content"><div class="section-wrapper page-full-width"><div class="section page-centered"><div><span>Kinsta is one of the top-managed </span><b>WordPress hosting companies,</b><span> with a growing club of 120,000+ businesses including startups, universities and Fortune 500 firms. We're motivated by excellent quality, and proud to have received a</span><b> 97% satisfaction rating from our customers</b><span>.</span></div><div><br></div><div><span>That's because, to us, each day is a chance to </span><b>grow</b><span>. As a team, we continuously inspire each other to learn new and better ways to make an impact. We own our jobs proudly - our leaders don't hold our hands, but they're in our corner whenever we ask for help - and our environment is </span><b>flexible</b><span> and </span><b>remote-first.</b></div><div><br></div><div><span>We’re looking for a results-driven<b> Product Marketing Manager</b> to take our go-to-market strategy to the next level. This role is perfect for someone who loves crafting compelling narratives, enabling sales and translating technical features into business value. As Product Marketing Manager, you’ll own the positioning, messaging and launch strategy for our products. You’ll be the critical bridge between product, marketing and sales—ensuring we deeply understand our buyers and deliver the right story to the right audience at the right time.</span></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>What You'll Do:</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>Define and maintain ideal customer profiles (ICPs) and buyer personas across segments and use cases.</li><li>Develop and refine product positioning, messaging and competitive differentiation based on customer insights, market trends and product capabilities.</li><li>Own go-to-market (GTM) strategy and execution for new features and product launches.</li><li>Develop and maintain compelling product narratives and collateral that clearly communicate the value proposition of our offerings.</li><li>Support the execution of global and regional go-to-market (GTM) initiativesCollaborate with Product, Sales, and Customer Success to create compelling sales enablement materials (pitch decks, battle cards, one-pagers).</li><li>Partner with Demand Gen and Content to fuel campaigns and content with product-specific messaging that drives pipeline.</li><li>Conduct customer research, win/loss analysis and competitor analysis to inform strategy and messaging.</li><li>Enable internal teams with training, messaging briefs and regular updates on product/market insights.</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>What You'll Bring to the role:</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>Bachelor’s degree in business, marketing or related field, MBA preferred</li><li>4–6+ years of experience in product marketing, ideally at a B2B SaaS company.</li><li>Strong strategic thinking and storytelling skills.</li><li>Experience launching products or features with measurable business impact.</li><li>Proven ability to translate complex technology into compelling, customer-facing narratives.</li><li>Excellent collaboration and communication skills across cross-functional teams.</li><li>Comfortable working in a fast-paced, growth-stage environment.</li><li>Experience with CRM and marketing tools (e.g., HubSpot, Salesforce, Google Analytics, etc.). </li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>Bonus points if you have:</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>Experience with PLG (product-led growth) or developer-focused SaaS.</li><li>A background in hosting technology or experience marketing to development personas.</li><li>Pragmatic Marketing certified.</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>Our Benefits: </h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li><b>Annual remote expense budge</b>t.</li><li><b>Flexible PTO.</b></li><li><b>Paid parental leave.</b></li><li><b>Annual professional development budget:</b> available after one year with Kinsta.</li><li><b>Sabbatical:</b> available after three years with Kinsta (and every three years thereafter).</li><li><b>Location-specific healthcare benefits</b> (including vision and dental) for employees hired in the USA, UK, and Hungary.</li><li><b>Pension plan</b> for employees hired in the UK, and 401k contributions for employees hired in the USA</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div>Kinsta is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We will not discriminate and we will take actions to ensure against discrimination in employment, recruitment, advertisements for employment, compensation, termination, promotions, and other conditions of employment against any employee or job applicant on the basis of race, color, gender, national origin, citizenship, age, religion, creed, disability, marital status, veteran’s status, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, or any other status protected by the laws or regulations in the locations where we operate.</div><div><br></div><div><span>By applying to one of our positions, you agree to the processing of the submitted personal data in accordance with Kinsta's </span><span>, including the transfer of your data to the United States.</span></div><div><br></div><div><i>#LI-Remote, #IHSRemote</i></div></div></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/kinsta-product-marketing-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/kinsta-product-marketing-manager</a></p>
</description>

      <expires_at>Fri, 29 May 2026 20:32:10 +0000</expires_at>

    <item>
      <title>Granicus: Product Manager</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> United Kingdom (Remote)
</p>

<div class="content"><div class="section-wrapper page-full-width"><div class="section page-centered"><div><b>The Company&nbsp;</b></div><div><b>Serving the People Who Serve the People&nbsp;</b></div><div>Granicus is driven by the excitement of building, implementing, and maintaining technology that is transforming the Govtech industry by bringing governments and their constituents together. We are on a mission to support our customers by meeting the needs of their communities and implementing our technology in ways that are equitable and inclusive. Granicus has consistently appeared on the GovTech 100 list over the past 5 years and has been recognized as the best companies to work on BuiltIn.&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div>Over the last 25 years, we have served 5,500 federal, state, and local government agencies and more than 300 million citizen subscribers powering an unmatched Subscriber Network that uses our digital solutions to make the world a better place. With comprehensive cloud-based solutions for communications, government website design, meeting and agenda management software, records management, and digital services, Granicus empowers stronger relationships between government and residents across the U.S., U.K., Australia, New Zealand, and Canada. By simplifying interactions with residents, while disseminating critical information, Granicus brings governments closer to the people they serve—driving meaningful change for communities around the globe.&nbsp;</div><div>Want to know more? See more of what we do <a class="postings-link" href="http://www.granicus.com/">here</a>.&nbsp;&nbsp;</div><div><br></div><div><span>Granicus is looking for a skilled Product Manager with deep interest in software and its ability to impact global democracy, citizen engagement, and increased government transparency. No company in the world reaches more people on behalf of public organizations as Granicus – and this individual will serve as both the internal and external evangelist for the solutions that make that happen. You possess a unique blend of business and technical savvy; a big-picture vision as well as the drive and attention to detail that transforms vision into a reality.</span></div><div><br></div><div><span>We’re looking for someone who realizes that success as a Product Manager is all about relationships. You will work closely with other Product Managers, Product Owners, Engineers, Designers, Data Analysts, and the Leadership team to define the future of Granicus Products. You are ultimately responsible for aligning the team behind the most important projects at any given time, with an eye toward driving growth in key metrics and constantly improving the user experience. Ideal candidates are extremely entrepreneurial and analytical, able to combine concrete data with solid instincts to move the product forward every day.</span></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>What your impact will look like:</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>Manage the long-term product vision and can translate that vision into tangible, actionable plans</li><li>Develop and execute specific product features, from ideation to implementation</li><li>Prioritize product deliverables against competing high-value features, clearly understanding tradeoffs</li><li>Utilize data-driven insights to decide when, what and how to deliver appropriate product scope</li><li>Proven ability to communicate, present, and sell your ideas to senior executives</li><li>Bring a detailed eye for user experience and design to ensure features drive high adoption, amazing quality and customer enthusiasm</li><li>Facilitate ideation within a specific product area through internal brainstorming, user outreach, feedback and usage data analysis, and intimate knowledge of the competitive landscape</li><li>Establish shared vision across the company by building consensus on priorities leading to product execution and launch</li><li>Support a team of world-class engineers, product owners and designers throughout product lifecycle</li><li>Define, analyze, and report on the metrics that determine the success of feature launches or experiments</li><li>Monitor adoption, usage and revenue impact of new services and enhancements and report on release impact as necessary</li><li>Engage with customers through a variety of channels and serve as the voice of the customer internally</li><li>Engage with the internal Implementation Services teams to understand, prioritize and productize specific customer needs.</li><li>Act as a mentor and resource to other Product team members</li><li>Participate in the continuous improvement and scaling of Product Management best practices at Granicus</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>Preferred qualifications:</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>4+ combined years in Product Management or equivalent experience guiding the development and maintenance of products</li><li>Fluency in JIRA and/or Aha!</li><li>Familiarity with the public sector </li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><h3>You'll love this job if you have:</h3><ul class="posting-requirements plain-list"><ul><li>Demonstrated ability to build quantitative business cases and financial models</li><li>Demonstrated experience with complex, transformational roadmap initiatives</li><li>Experience delivering high-quality technology products/services in a fast-moving environment where priorities and structures shift rapidly</li><li>Success working in an agile environment</li><li>High level of intellectual curiosity drives you to ask great questions and seek supporting data to interpret the most important needs of the product</li><li>Excellent organizational and quantitative analytical skills</li><li>Technical depth that enables you to understand and earn the respect of the engineers you work with</li><li>Ability to break the long-term vision down into specific, manageable deliverables that deliver user value as quickly as possible</li><li>Ability to think strategically with the capacity to expand customer use cases into platform-wide requirements</li><li>Demonstrated success in working with cross-functional teams</li><li>Excellent communication skills regardless of the audience — software engineers, product designers, business stakeholders, users, etc.</li></ul></ul></div></div><div class="section page-centered"><div><br></div><div><span>Don’t have all the skills/experience mentioned above? At Granicus, we are trying to build diverse, inclusive teams. We do not have degree requirements for most of our roles. If you don’t meet every requirement above but are excited to learn more, we encourage you to apply. We might just be able to find another role that could be a perfect fit!&nbsp;</span></div><div><br></div><div><b><span>Security and Privacy Requirements</span></b></div><div><span>-&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Responsible for Granicus information security by appropriately preserving the Confidentiality, Integrity, and Availability (CIA) of Granicus information assets in accordance with the company's information security program.</span></div><div><span>-&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;Responsible for ensuring the data privacy of our employees and customers, their data, as well as taking all required privacy training in a timely manner, in accordance with company policies.</span></div><div><br></div><div><b><span>The Team</span></b></div><div><span>- We are a remote-first company with a globally distributed workforce across the United States, Canada, United Kingdom, India, Armenia, Australia, and New Zealand.</span></div><div><br></div><div><b><span>The Culture</span></b></div><div><span>- At Granicus, we are building a transparent, inclusive, and safe space for everyone who wants to be</span></div><div><span>a part of our journey.</span></div><div><span>- A few culture highlights include –&nbsp;Employee Resource Groups to encourage diverse voices</span></div><div><span>- Coffee with Mark sessions – Our employees get to interact with our CEO on very important and</span></div><div><span>sometimes difficult issues ranging from mental health to work-life balance and current affairs.&nbsp;</span></div><div><span>- Microsoft Teams communities focused on wellness, art, furbabies, family, parenting, and more.-=- - We bring in special guests from time to time to discuss issues that impact our employee</span></div><div><span>population&nbsp;</span></div><div><br></div><div><b><span>The Impact</span></b></div><div><span>- We are proud to serve dynamic organizations around the globe that use our digital solutions to make the world a better place — quite literally.&nbsp;We have so many powerful success stories that illustrate how our solutions are impacting the world. See more of our impact&nbsp;</span><a href="https://granicus.com/success-stories/"><span>here</span></a><span>.</span></div><div><br></div><div><i>Granicus is committed to providing equal employment opportunities. All qualified applicants and employees will be considered for employment and advancement without regard to race, color, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, gender identity, gender expression, physical or mental disability, age, genetic information, sexual or affectional orientation, marital status, status regarding public assistance, familial status, military or veteran status or any other status protected by applicable law.</i></div></div></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/granicus-product-manager">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/granicus-product-manager</a></p>
</description>

    <item>
      <title>Bonial International: Senior Product Manager (f/m/x)</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state></state>
      <skills></skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Hussitenstraße 32-33 Berlin, BE, 13355 Germany
</p>

<div class="sc-1gch2qa-0 fhKCjg"><div><div class="sc-gfrfnr-2 deOrHQ"></div><div class="sc-18h7are-0 ebVyJP"><div class="sc-1tu9fip-2 ezRMeN"><div class="sc-1tu9fip-1 kdUgoN"><div class="sc-1tu9fip-3 iFQjUO"><div class="sc-ji5wy4-0 jIYAZB"><div><div><div><div><div class="sc-1uwf3m5-2 ipgOZn"><div class="sc-1v95195-0 jscoSJ"><div><div><h2 class="sc-1npqnwg-4 sc-1npqnwg-5 sc-1uwf3m5-0 gkPLZR custom-css-style-job-description">Jobbeschreibung</h2><div class="sc-1fwbcuw-0 iMQxSN"><div><p><strong><span>bonial hilft Dir dabei Zeit, Geld und Papier zu sparen.</span></strong></p><p><span>Wir sind ein digitaler Werbepartner für das Offline-Business. Wir unterstützen Händler:innen in deren Marketing-Aktivitäten und helfen dabei, neue Kund:innen zu gewinnen.</span></p><p></p><p><strong><span>Würdest Du gern dazugehören und Teil des digitalen Wandels im Handel sein?</span></strong></p><p><span>bonial ist Teil der </span><strong><span>Axel Springer SE </span></strong><span>und ist der führende “Drive to Store”-Partner in Deutschland und Frankreich. Wir inspirieren täglich Millionen von Nutzer:innen, indem wir sie mit ihren Lieblingshändlern und -marken durch "</span><strong><a href="https://www.kaufda.de/webapp/?lat=49.452103&amp;lng=11.076665"><span>kaufDA</span></a></strong><span>" und "</span><strong><a href="https://www.meinprospekt.de/"><span>MeinProspekt</span></a></strong><span>" in Deutschland, und “</span><strong><a href="https://www.bonial.fr/webapp/"><span>bonial</span></a></strong><span>” in Frankreich verbinden.</span></p><p></p><p></p><p></p><p><span>Wir suchen eine dynamische und ergebnisorientierte Produktmanager:in mit datengesteuerter und pragmatischer Denkweise zur Verstärkung unseres Produktmanagement Teams. Als zukünftige*r Produktmanager:in im Client-Facing Produktteam arbeitest Du eng mit den Vertriebs- und operativen Teams zusammen, um ein erstklassiges Onboarding-Erlebnis für Kampagnen sicherzustellen. Du und Dein Team seid verantwortlich für die Entwicklung skalierbarer und effizienter Lösungen, um Inhalte von unseren Kund:innen zu empfangen und diese für die weitere Content-Erstellung zu transformieren.</span></p><p></p><p><span>Die ideale Kandidat:in bringt umfassende Erfahrung in der Produktverantwortung mit: komplexe Probleme gemeinsam mit dem Team zu lösen, Ideen zur Zielerreichung zu identifizieren und zu bewerten, die eigene Domäne in die Gesamtvision einzubetten, proaktiv organisationsweit zu kollaborieren, Veränderungen aktiv zu gestalten und in Unsicherheit handlungsfähig zu bleiben.</span></p><p></p><p><strong><span>Aufgaben</span></strong></p><ul><li><p><span>Verantwortung für die maximale Wertschöpfun</span><span>g im Bereich Content-Sourcing</span></p></li><li><p><span>Enge Zusammenarbeit mit Engineering-Team, Designer:innen und Stakeholdern, um sicherzustellen, dass „die richtigen Dinge richtig gebaut werden“</span></p></li><li><p><span>Überblick behalten und sicherstellen, dass Initiativen mit den Unternehmenszielen übereinstimmen</span></p></li><li><p><span>Übersetzung der Produktstrategie in klar priorisierte Maßnahmen auf Ebene von Roadmaps, Initiativen, Epics und User Stories</span></p></li><li><p><span>Identifikation und Priorisierung von Chancen durch Datenanalyse und User Research, um Geschäftsbedürfnisse zu adressieren und Nutzer:innen echten Mehrwert zu bieten</span></p></li><li><p><span>Hypothesen definieren und (in-)validieren sowie Metriken zur Erfolgsmessung von Ergebnissen definieren</span></p></li><li><p><span>Ideen in Initiativen mit klar definierten </span><span>Anwendungsfällen</span><span>, Szenarien und Akzeptanzkriterien überführen</span></p></li><li><p><span>Zusammenarbeit mit anderen Product Manager:innen und Engineering-Teams, um die operativen Prozesse optimal zu gestalten</span></p></li><li><p><span>Unterstützung von Kund:innen beim Onboarding und bei Koordinationsgesprächen, um eine reibungslose Datenintegration sicherzustellen</span></p></li></ul></div></div><h2 class="sc-1npqnwg-4 sc-1npqnwg-5 sc-1uwf3m5-0 sc-1uwf3m5-1 fHjUAo custom-css-style-job-requirements">Stellenanforderungen</h2><div class="sc-1fwbcuw-0 iMQxSN"><div><ul><li><p><strong><span>Datengetriebene, pragmatische und zielorientierte Denkweise</span></strong><span>: Du triffst Entscheidungen auf Basis von Daten, formulierst klare Ziele und definierst passende Erfolgskennzahlen.</span></p></li><li><p><strong><span>Problemlösungskompetenz</span></strong><span>: Du erkennst Chancen oder Probleme durch Daten oder Nutzerforschung, brichst komplexe Sachverhalte auf verständliche Komponenten herunter und entwickelst frühzeitig eine Strategie zur Validierung von Hypothesen.</span></p></li><li><p><strong><span>Starke Priorisierungsfähigkeit</span></strong><span>: Du priorisierst Möglichkeiten entlang des Business Impacts und strategischer Zielsetzungen.</span></p></li><li><p><strong><span>Strategisches Denken</span></strong><span>: Du erkennst Zusammenhänge zwischen Themenfeldern, verstehst Zielbilder und leitest Wege zur Zielerreichung ab.</span></p></li><li><p><strong><span>Business-Verständnis</span></strong><span>: Du kannst den ROI von Initiativen bewerten und fundierte Business Cases aufstellen.</span></p></li><li><p><strong><span>Veränderungsmanagement</span></strong><span>: Du bringst nachweisbare Erfahrung in der erfolgreichen Begleitung von organisatorischem Wandel mit – idealerweise im technischen oder plattformbezogenen Umfeldern.</span></p></li><li><p><strong><span>Exzellente Kommunikationsfähigkeiten</span></strong><span>, zugeschnitten auf verschiedene Zielgruppen (Kund:innen, Business, Nutzer:innen &amp; Stakeholder, Tech-Team).</span></p></li><li><p><strong><span>AI-Kompetenz</span></strong><span>: Du hast ein praktisches Verständnis von Chancen und Grenzen von KI, identifizierst sinnvolle Anwendungen, nutzt KI-Tools im Arbeitsalltag und arbeitest effektiv mit Engineering- und Data-Teams an AI-Initiativen.</span></p></li><li><p><strong><span>Erfahrung im Plattform-Produktmanagement</span></strong><span>: Du bringst nachweisbare Erfahrung als </span><strong><span>Product Owner oder Product Manager</span></strong><span>, idealerweise mit Fokus auf technische oder Plattformprodukte, mit.</span></p></li><li><p><span>Erfahrung in der </span><strong><span>Entwicklung interner Tools</span></strong><span> ist von Vorteil.</span></p></li><li><p><span>Sprachkenntnisse: </span><strong><span>Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse</span></strong><span> erforderlich; Französisch ist von Vorteil.</span></p></li></ul><p></p><p><strong><span>Was wir Dir bieten können:</span></strong></p><p><strong><span>Diversität</span></strong><span> - Wir sind stolz darauf, ein Arbeitsplatz für Chancengleichheit zu sein, an dem wir es jeder Person ermöglichen möchten, ihr wahres selbst zu zeigen. Wir setzten uns für gleiche Beschäftigungsmöglichkeiten ein, und dafür, ein Safe Space zu sein unabhängig von ethnischer Herkunft, Religion, Geschlecht, sexueller Orientierung, Alter, Behinderung, Gender, Geschlechtsidentität oder Geschlechtsausdruck.</span></p><p><strong><span>Entwicklung</span></strong><span> - Unterstützung für Deine persönliche Entwicklung durch eine Feedback-Kultur, die Dir hilft, Deine Stärken und Wachstumspotenzial zu identifizieren.</span></p><p><strong><span>Wohlbefinden</span></strong><span> - Für uns ist mentale Gesundheit genauso wichtig wie Fitness. Deshalb bieten wir Dir einen kostenlosen Zugang zu nilo.health, einer App für mentale Gesundheit, an. Außerdem brauchst Du Dir keine Sorgen um Deine Rente machen, denn wir bezuschussen Deine betriebliche Altersvorsorge. Zusätzlich zu unserem internen Fitnesstudio, übernehmen wir auch einen Teil der Kosten für Urban Sports Club.</span></p><p><strong><span>Nachhaltigkeit</span></strong><span> - Dir liegt das Thema Nachhaltigkeit am Herzen? Uns auch! Zusammen mit unserem Green Team bieten wir Dir die Möglichkeit, Dich in Projekten zu Nachhaltigkeit und Umweltschutz zu engagieren. Gemeinsam wollen wir unseren Beitrag leisten und den Planeten so positiv beeinflussen, wie wir nur können.</span></p><p><strong><span>Flexibilität</span></strong><span> - wir bieten flexible Arbeitszeiten und Möglichkeit, vom Ausland aus zu arbeiten.&nbsp;Wir bieten 28 Urlaubstage an, die sich um einen Tag pro gearbeitetes Kalenderjahr (bis zu max. 30 Tage) erhöhen. Zusätzliche Tage gibt es für spezielle Anlässe wie z.B. einen Umzug, Teilnahmen an sozialen Projekten, etc.</span></p><p><strong><span>Modernes</span></strong><span> </span><strong><span>Büro</span></strong><span> - Wir haben ein eigenes und voll ausgestattetes Fitnessstudio, eine Dachterrasse für Team Events und Mittagspausen in der Sonne, sowie Zen Rooms, in denen Mitarbeitende beten, entspannen oder einfach eine ruhige Minute finden können.</span></p><p><strong><span>Kostenloses</span></strong><span> </span><strong><span>Mittagessen</span></strong><span>&nbsp;- Wenn Du im Büro bist, bieten wir Dir verschiedene Optionen für ein Mittagessen an, und das Mittagessen geht auf uns! Du kannst in dem nahliegenden Partner-Restaurant essen gehen! Natürlich sind auch heiße sowie kalte Getränke, Obst und andere Snacks im Büro für unsere Mitarbeiter:innen kostenlos.</span></p><p><strong><span>Soziale Kultur</span></strong><span> - Ein gutes Miteinander ist uns wichtig, weshalb wir Wert auf eine Kultur legen, die dazu einlädt, miteinander ins Gespräch zu kommen, gute Arbeitsbeziehungen aufzubauen und sich gemeinschaftlich an Projekten zu beteiligen. Wir bieten außerdem regelmäßig optionale Events an, um dies weiter zu unterstützen.</span></p><p></p><p><strong><span>Hört sich das gut an für Dich? Wir freuen uns von Dir zu hören!</span></strong></p><p></p><p><span>Besuche uns gerne auch auf unserer </span><a href="https://www.instagram.com/bonial_people/?hl=en"><span>bonial_people</span></a><span> Instagram Seite oder erfahre mehr über unsere bonial-Kultur unter </span><a href="https://medium.com/insidebonial"><span>bonial Medium</span></a><span>.</span></p></div></div></div></div><div class="sc-1uwf3m5-4 fKDjfR"><div class="sc-e5uvhz-0 kHqIkC"><div class="sc-e5uvhz-8 ekemGm"></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div></div><div><div><div class="sc-1f8x0pm-0 ibrjJt"><div><h3 class="sc-1npqnwg-4 sc-1npqnwg-5 sc-1f8x0pm-1 jgoXVa">Alles erledigt!</h3><p class="sc-1npqnwg-9 sc-1npqnwg-10 iuofcq">Ihre Bewerbung wurde eingesendet!</p></div><div class="sc-1f8x0pm-2 dvyFlr"><a class="sc-s03za1-0 cmZAOR custom-css-style-application-success-button" href="/">Anderer Job </a></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/bonial-international-senior-product-manager-f-m-x">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/bonial-international-senior-product-manager-f-m-x</a></p>
</description>
