      <title>EngagedMD: Staff Software Engineer</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country>🇺🇸 United States of America</country>
      <state>District of Columbia</state>
      <skills>GraphQL, Node.js, React, and TypeScript</skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0123/5852/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Washington, DC
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://engaged-md.com">https://engaged-md.com</a>
</p>

<p>You’re an experienced engineer with exceptional skill and leadership ability, capable of making an impact across our product ecosystem. We’re EngagedMD and we have a patient journey application used by more than 3 million users nationwide and internationally since its launch. We continue to see enormous growth and adoption from medical clinics across multiple markets. This is an exciting opportunity to join us in our mission to help carers carry on caring.&nbsp;</p>
<p>You will have the opportunity to architect backend infrastructure and develop new features for our customers while also closely collaborating with our Platform, Product and Customer Success teams.</p>
<p>This fully remote role reports to our Director of Engineering and candidates are required to reside in the United States. This role requires legal authorization to work in the United States. EngagedMD is unable to sponsor current H1B visa holders for this role.&nbsp;</p>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>What You’ll Do</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Design, build and maintain backend services and APIs using TypeScript and modern Node.js frameworks</li>
<li>Lead system design efforts for new and existing platforms, including service decomposition, data modeling, scalability and reliability considerations</li>
<li>Own complex backend domains end-to-end, from initial architecture through implementation, deployment and long-term maintenance</li>
<li>Drive technical direction and standards across the backend stack, including coding practices, service boundaries, observability, error handling and performance</li>
<li>Make pragmatic architectural tradeoffs that balance delivery speed with system quality and long-term maintainability</li>
<li>Collaborate closely with product, frontend, and infrastructure teams to deliver scalable, reliable product capabilities</li>
<li>Identify and proactively address technical debt, scalability bottlenecks, security concerns and operational risks</li>
<li>Mentor and support engineers through code reviews, design discussions and hands-on technical guidance</li>
<li>Contribute to incident response, root-cause analysis and improvements to system resiliency</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>Technical Focus Areas</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>TypeScript and Node.js backend development</li>
<li>API design (REST, GraphQL, or equivalent)</li>
<li>Databases, schema design and data consistency</li>
<li>Distributed systems fundamentals (scalability, fault tolerance, eventual consistency)</li>
<li>Cloud-based deployments and production operations</li>
</ul>
<p><strong>What You’ll Bring</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>8+ years of professional software engineering experience, with significant backend focus</li>
<li>Deep expertise in TypeScript and building production backend services</li>
<li>Strong system design skills, with experience designing and evolving large-scale or business-critical systems</li>
<li>Proven ability to operate autonomously and make sound technical decisions in ambiguous environments</li>
<li>Experience balancing long-term architectural quality with short-term delivery needs</li>
<li>Strong communication skills; able to clearly explain technical concepts to engineers and non-engineers</li>
<li>A mission-driven orientation to all you do&nbsp;</li>
<li>Ability to thrive in a small, fast-paced organization&nbsp;</li>
<li>Bachelor’s degree or equivalent career experience&nbsp;</li>
</ul>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>It’s Also Nice If You…</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Have at least a passing familiarity with the fertility industry and/or experience in healthtech with a focus on patient journey software</li>
<li>Have successfully worked remotely with distributed teams in the past</li>
</ul>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>What We Offer</strong></p>
<ul>
<li>Competitive compensation and equity</li>
<li>Medical, dental, and vision coverage heavily subsidized by the company</li>
<li>Open Paid Time Off</li>
<li>Paid family and medical leave</li>
<li>Generous paid holidays</li>
<li>401(k) retirement savings plan&nbsp;</li>
</ul>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p><em>EngagedMD is an equal opportunity employer. We’re committed to diversity, equity, and inclusion. We believe our company and products are made better by the varied perspectives of our employees, each of whom we seek to empower and support in their individuality. We hire our team members on the basis of merit, qualifications, and the needs of our business in order to best achieve our mission to help carers carry on caring. Pursuant to this commitment, EngagedMD will ensure that persons with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodations for the hiring process. If reasonable accommodation is needed, please contact&nbsp;</em><a href="mailto:peopleops@engagedmd.com"><em>peopleops@engagedmd.com</em></a>.</p>
<p><br><br></p>
<p><strong>About EngagedMD</strong></p>
<p>At EngagedMD, we embrace a mission-driven culture where committed individuals come together to make a real impact in healthcare. Our core values of integrity, collaboration, impact, recognition and growth inform how we work together. They also make possible our culture of belonging and excellence where our team members can be themselves, grow professionally, do their best work, and be rewarded for it. Join us today in our mission to help carers carry on caring.</p>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/engagedmd-staff-software-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/engagedmd-staff-software-engineer</a></p>

      <country>🇦🇱 Albania, 🇦🇹 Austria, 🇧🇪 Belgium, 🇧🇬 Bulgaria, 🇨🇾 Cyprus, 🇨🇿 Czechia, 🇩🇪 Germany, 🇩🇰 Denmark, 🇪🇪 Estonia, 🇪🇸 Spain, 🇫🇮 Finland, 🇫🇷 France, 🇬🇷 Greece, 🇭🇷 Croatia, 🇭🇺 Hungary, 🇮🇪 Ireland, 🇮🇹 Italy, 🇱🇹 Lithuania, 🇱🇺 Luxembourg, 🇱🇻 Latvia, 🇲🇹 Malta, 🇳🇱 Netherlands, 🇵🇱 Poland, 🇵🇹 Portugal, 🇷🇴 Romania, 🇸🇪 Sweden, 🇸🇮 Slovenia, and 🇸🇰 Slovakia</country>
      <state>New York</state>

      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>

      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0018/9370/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> New York, NY
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://www.stickermule.com">https://www.stickermule.com</a>
</p>

<p><strong>About Sticker Mule</strong></p>
<p>Sticker Mule, the Internet's favorite custom printer, is building the Internet's best commerce platform. We are privately-owned, which means no meddlesome investors, with 9-figures in annual revenue and great profitability. Our commerce platform (<a class="jsx-2845998404 link primary" href="https://www.stickermule.com/stores/learn-more">Stores)</a>&nbsp;helped people earn $200k while in beta and now we are hiring to accelerate development.</p>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>We offer</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>
<p>Remote work with flexible schedules</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>A privately owned, low-stress culture</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>A fun "no bullshit" work environment</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Great compensation with rising wages.</p>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p><strong>Compensation and benefits</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>
<p>Salary: $145K&nbsp;</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>$20,000 signing bonus</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>4 weeks vacation + holidays based on your country of residence</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>Requirements</strong></p>
<ol>
<li>
<p>Go</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>GraphQL</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Postgres</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Redis</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Docker</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Kubernetes</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Google Cloud Platform</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Excellent communication skills (English)</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Degree in Computer Science or equivalent practical experience</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Familiarity with Typescript</p>
</li>
<li>
<p>Availability to travel to our factory in Italy a few times a year</p>
</li>
</ol>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p><strong>What you will work on</strong></p>
<p>You'll help us develop our new&nbsp;<a class="jsx-2845998404 link primary" href="https://www.stickermule.com/stores/learn-more">Stores</a>&nbsp;platform that already helped sellers earn nearly $200,000 while in beta. Stores is supported by a suite of tools that help anyone succeed online including&nbsp;<a class="jsx-2845998404 link primary" href="https://www.stickermule.com/give">Give</a>&nbsp;(our giveaway platform),&nbsp;<a class="jsx-2845998404 link primary" href="https://www.stickermule.com/ship">Ship</a>&nbsp;(our shipping platform, Notify (our forthcoming email/sms marketing platform), and more coming soon.</p>
<p>&nbsp;</p>
<p>We have an incredible roadmap and need your help to execute it quickly.</p>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/sticker-mule-software-engineer-2">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/sticker-mule-software-engineer-2</a></p>
</description>

      <title>CodeSignal: Software Engineer, Business Experience</title>
      <region>Anywhere in the World</region>
      <country></country>
      <state>California</state>
      <skills>AWS, JavaScript, MongoDB, MySQL, Node.js, PostgreSQL, Preact.js, and TypeScript</skills>
      <category>Full-Stack Programming</category>
      <type>Full-Time</type>
      <description><img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/2586/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" />

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> San Francisco
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://codesignal.com/">https://codesignal.com/</a>
</p>

<div>We are looking for a Software Engineer who wants to be an impactful part of a small team looking to change the way technical hiring is done. In this role, you will be an integral part of our Product Engineering team and will drive features with immediate business impact to our core products:&nbsp;<a href="https://codesignal.com/products/pre-screen/">Assessments</a>,<a href="https://codesignal.com/products/interview/">&nbsp;Interviews</a>, and&nbsp;<a href="https://codesignal.com/learn/course-paths">Learn</a>. You’ll be a key member of the team that builds and maintains our identity and authentication systems, tying all of our products together.</div>
<div>To learn more about the kinds of projects we work on, check out our<a href="https://codesignal.com/blog/engineering/">&nbsp;</a><a href="https://codesignal.com/blog/engineering/">Engineering blog</a>! Some noteworthy projects include:</div>
<ul>
<li><a href="https://codesignal.com/blog/engineering/why-we-moved-from-mongodb-meteor-observers-to-redis/">Why we moved from MongoDB &amp; Meteor observers to Redis</a></li>
<li><a href="https://codesignal.com/blog/engineering/what-we-learned-when-10000-users-hit-our-platform-at-once/">What we learned when 10,000 users hit our platform at once</a></li>
<li><a href="https://codesignal.com/blog/engineering/how-to-avoid-issues-with-your-html-email-formatting/">How to avoid issues with your HTML email formatting</a></li>
</ul>
<div><strong>Day-to-Day&nbsp;</strong></div>
<ul>
<li>Contribute to building product features across the full stack (from the front-end to the back-end to separate microservices)</li>
<li>Develop clean and maintainable code, with an emphasis on quality and craftsmanship</li>
<li>Conduct code reviews and collaborate with your peers</li>
<li>Lead technical design discussions on features where you're able to lend your expertise</li>
</ul>
<div><strong>Basic Qualifications</strong></div>
<ul>
<li>At least 4 years of professional, full-time software engineering experience</li>
<li>Clear and effective communication skills</li>
<li>Drive and initiative for getting things done</li>
<li>Experience with TypeScript/JavaScript (browser, Node, or both)</li>
<li>Available to work full-time. Candidates must be based within GMT-GMT+6 timezones.</li>
</ul>
<div><strong>Bonus Points for the Following</strong></div>
<ul>
<li>Experience with any part of our stack:</li>
<li>React</li>
<li>Node</li>
<li>MongoDB</li>
<li>GraphQL</li>
<li>Experience with SSO, SCIM or RBAC.</li>
<li>Any experience in a related industry, including assessments, HR tech, or education.</li>
</ul>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/codesignal-software-engineer-business-experience-1">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/codesignal-software-engineer-business-experience-1</a></p>
</description>

<p>
  <strong>Headquarters:</strong> London
    <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://joinstellar.ai/">https://joinstellar.ai/</a>
</p>

<div class="position-content">
<div class="position-description">We are seeking experienced Software Engineers to contribute to projects across a wide range of technologies and programming languages, including JavaScript, Python, Go, C++, Ruby, and more.<br>This is an open-ended contract opportunity, structured around project-based work with no set schedules or minimum time or task commitments. Contributors who work with us decide which available projects they'd like to work on, when they work, and how much they contribute, which makes it an excellent option for those seeking flexible and high-impact work.<br>In this role, you will help advance AI research by understanding complex systems, exploring large codebases, and developing approaches to verify the correctness of software behavior.<br>
<h3>Compensation</h3>
<ul>
<li>$70 per hour after passing the qualification</li>
<li>Fully remote</li>
<li>Project based work, no minimum time or task commitments</li>
</ul>
<h3>Responsibilities</h3>
<ul>
<li>Rapidly build context in large, multi-module codebases</li>
<li>Analyze feature implementations for correctness and edge cases</li>
<li>Produce clear, structured documentation of findings and rationale</li>
</ul>
<h3>Requirements</h3>
<ul>
<li>Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field</li>
<li>At least 2 years of experience as a Software Engineer</li>
<li>Excellent written communication skills</li>
<li>Ability to clearly explain complex technical concepts</li>
</ul>
<h3>Location</h3>
<p>Remote in the US, UK, Canada, Europe, Australia, or New Zealand</p>
</div>
</div>

<p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/stellar-ai-senior-software-engineer">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/stellar-ai-senior-software-engineer</a></p>

<div>Imagine choosing your projects, collaborating with the best, and keeping every dollar you earn.<br><br></div><div><br>At A·Team, we’ve turned this vision into reality for top-tier independent software builders.
